rt_critics_consensus: Poignant documentary that's hard to forget.

title: The Birdcage
rt_critics_consensus: Mike Nichols wrangles agreeably amusing performances from Robin Williams and Nathan Lane in this fun, if not quite essential, remake of the French comedy La Cage aux Folles.

title: Redline
rt_critics_consensus: Redline has plenty of bad acting, laughable dialogue, and luxury cars.

title: Darkman II: The Return of Durant
rt_critics_consensus: Without the gothic melodrama and berserk wit of its predecessor, Darkman II: The Return of Durant struggles to rise above its made-for-video roots despite flashes of fun and admirable cinematic ambition.

title: Nekrotronic
rt_critics_consensus: Nekrotonic has plenty of oddball energy, but this horror-comedy hybrid mashes up ingredients without much of a clear idea of what to do with them.

title: Renfield
rt_critics_consensus: Although it fails to take full advantage of its committed stars and killer premise, Renfield's batty horror-comedy blend sinks in just enough to leave an impression.

title: The Angry Black Girl and Her Monster
rt_critics_consensus: Injecting a classic story with fresh innovation and social relevance, The Angry Black Girl and Her Monster is a thrillingly assured feature debut for writer-director Bomani J. Story.

title: The Werewolf Game: The Villagers Side
The Swerve
rt_critics_consensus: Bound together by Azura Skye's riveting work in the central role, The Swerve paints a harrowing portrait of a life unmoored.

title: The Page Turner
rt_critics_consensus: Reminiscent of Hitchcock and Chabrol, The Page Turner is elegant yet suspenseful, a revenge potboiler of a high degree.

title: ABCs of Death 2
rt_critics_consensus: ABCs of Death 2 delivers some inventively gory thrills, offering a surprising (albeit still somewhat uneven) upgrade over its predecessor.

title: November
rt_critics_consensus: Murky and too artsy for its own good, November ends up being a case of style with little substance.

title: A Ghost Love Story of Japan
Didn't Die
rt_critics_consensus: Didn't Die mixes lo-fi visuals, humor, and horror effectively, but uneven pacing and a slight premise keep its drama from fully landing.

title: Reverb
rt_critics_consensus: Badly written and directed, this poorly executed British horror suffers a glaring lack of scares and comprehensibility.

title: The Conjuring
rt_critics_consensus: Well-crafted and gleefully creepy, The Conjuring ratchets up dread through a series of effective old-school scares.

title: Hellbender
rt_critics_consensus: Hellbender stirs a boiling cauldron of adolescent angst, serving up a coming-of-age story with dark horror overtones.

title: The Haunting of Hell Hole Mine
Adult Swim Yule Log 2: Branchin' Out
Taken
rt_critics_consensus: Taken is undeniably fun with slick action, but is largely a brainless exercise.

title: Chabak - Night of Murder and Romance
The Sacrament
rt_critics_consensus: While it may be a bit too slow for some, The Sacrament offers enough tense atmosphere and intriguing ideas to satisfy discerning horror buffs.

title: The Woman in the Yard
rt_critics_consensus: The Woman in the Yard has plenty of spooky promise in its premise and a committed performance from Danielle Deadwyler, but the story's heavy-handed metaphor leaves little room for scares or surprise.

title: Holes in the Sky: The Sean Miller Story
Synchronic
rt_critics_consensus: Synchronic sets off on an intriguingly idiosyncratic sci-fi journey that should satisfy fans of Aaron Moorhead and Justin Benson's earlier work.

title: House Party
rt_critics_consensus: House Party is a light, entertaining teen comedy with an infectious energy.

title: Thus Spoke Kishibe Rohan: At a Confessional
Terrifier 3
rt_critics_consensus: Taking an axe to yuletide cheer and everything else in its path, Terrifier 3 is a bloody fantastic stocking stuffer for the holiday season.

title: A Melty Kiss Lost in the Abyss
Abraham's Boys: A Dracula Story
rt_critics_consensus: Abrahams Boys: A Dracula Story bleeds its source material dry, opting for insipid plotting and misleading story-beats.

title: The Perfection
rt_critics_consensus: Led by a pair of compelling performances, The Perfection is a smart, gripping thriller that barbs its wild twists with cutting wit.

title: Paranormal Activity: The Marked Ones
rt_critics_consensus: A change of setting breathes some new life into the franchise, but Paranormal Activity: The Marked Ones fails to provide enough consistent thrills to justify a fifth film in the series.

title: M3GAN
rt_critics_consensus: Unapologetically silly and all the more entertaining for it, M3GAN is the rare horror-comedy that delivers chuckles as effortlessly as chills.

title: See for Me
rt_critics_consensus: A home invasion thriller with a nifty twist, See for Me is further elevated by Skyler Davenport's magnetic lead performance.

title: Beauty and the Beast
rt_critics_consensus: With its magical optical effects and enchanting performances, Jean Cocteau's Beauty and the Beast remains the most surreal -- and soulful -- of the fairy tale's film adaptations.

title: Carnage for Christmas
rt_critics_consensus: Elevated by Jeremy Moineau's standout performance and Alice Maio Mackay's distinctive direction, Carnage for Christmas unwraps a visually striking, fun whodunnit.

title: Slotherhouse
rt_critics_consensus: As bonkers as a killer arboreal edentate mammal, Slotherhouse delivers B-movie thrills to gore lovers and Greek life haters alike.

title: Ghost Killer
rt_critics_consensus: Ghost Killer embraces its offbeat premise with confidence, delivering slick action, playful chemistry, and earnest charm.

title: Dominion: Prequel to the Exorcist
rt_critics_consensus: While director Schrader's attempt at a literate, internal exposition on evil temptations and human sin is admirable, this prequel suffers from hit-and-miss psychological tension, poor visual effects, and weak writing -- an overambitious failure of a horror movie.

title: Get Out
rt_critics_consensus: Funny, scary, and thought-provoking, Get Out seamlessly weaves its trenchant social critiques into a brilliantly effective and entertaining horror/comedy thrill ride.

title: The Return of the Prodigal Son
Unfriended
rt_critics_consensus: Unfriended subverts found-footage horror clichés to deliver a surprisingly scary entry in the teen slasher genre with a technological twist.

title: World War Dead: Rise of the Fallen
Fracture
rt_critics_consensus: Though Fracture's plot is somewhat implausible, the onscreen face-off between Gosling and Hopkins overshadows any faults.

title: Fractured
rt_critics_consensus: Led by an arresting Sam Worthington performance, Fractured is a reasonably diverting mystery with enough thrills to make up for a familiar story.

title: The Ghost of Sierra de Cobre
Children of the Corn V: Fields of Terror
Blood and Chocolate
rt_critics_consensus: Cheap CG effects and laughable dialogue make Blood and Chocolate worse than the usual werewolf flick.

title: Hide
rt_critics_consensus: Cleverly written and directed, this is a tight, atmospheric and claustrophobic low budget British thriller.

title: 30 Minutes or Less
rt_critics_consensus: It's sporadically funny and it benefits from a talented cast, but 30 Minutes or Less suffers from a disjointed narrative, and too often mistakes crude gags for true lowbrow humor.

title: Witchcraft XII: In the Lair of the Serpent
They Don't Cut the Grass Anymore
Scream VI
rt_critics_consensus: Certain aspects of horror's most murderously meta franchise may be going stale, but a change of setting and some inventive set pieces help keep Scream VI reasonably sharp.

title: We Need to Do Something
rt_critics_consensus: While We Need to Do Something can feel as unfocused as its title, it offers eerily timely genre thrills, soaked in claustrophobic dread.

title: The Carpenter's Son
rt_critics_consensus: The Carpenter's Son is peculiar enough to hold some cult appeal, but for all the promise this biblical horror's conception holds, its execution proves far from immaculate.

title: The Innkeepers
rt_critics_consensus: It doesn't break any rules of the genre, but The Innkeepers serves as additional proof that Ti West is a young director that discriminating horror fans can trust.

title: Z
rt_critics_consensus: Powerfully effective, this anti-fascist political thriller stands out as both high-conscience melodrama and high-tempo action movie.

title: Nocebo
rt_critics_consensus: Nocebo struggles to consistently engage, but unsettling atmosphere and interesting ideas make this thriller difficult to dismiss outright.

title: Four Unloved Women, Adrift on a Purposeless Sea, Experience the Ecstasy of Dissection
Son of Frankenstein
rt_critics_consensus: Boris Karloff's final appearance as the Monster is a fitting farewell before the series descended into self-parody.

title: The Last Sharknado: It's About Time
She Will
rt_critics_consensus: More unsettling than truly scary, She Will casts a thoughtful, hypnotic spell made even stronger by its restrained approach and some strong performances.

title: The Lodge
rt_critics_consensus: Led by an impressive Riley Keough performance, The Lodge should prove a suitably unsettling destination for fans of darkly atmospheric horror.

title: Found Footage of Fear: Digital Terror
Constantine
rt_critics_consensus: Despite solid production values and an intriguing premise, Constantine lacks the focus of another spiritual shoot-em-up: The Matrix.

title: For God's Sake Wake Her Up
Instinct
rt_critics_consensus: A convoluted and predictable plot overshadows the performances.

title: The Forever Purge
rt_critics_consensus: The Forever Purge fails to fully engage with its most frighteningly timely themes, but the franchise remains largely -- albeit bluntly -- effective.

title: Archipelago
rt_critics_consensus: Sad, funny, and wise in equal measure, Archipelago finds writer-director Joanna Hogg in remarkably strong, confident form.

title: The House That Would Not Die
LGBT: Lethal Gay Butcher of Terror
My Uncle John Is a Zombie!
When Evil Lurks
rt_critics_consensus: A hard-hitting horror film whose surface-level frights are just as engaging as its thematic concerns, When Evil Lurks marks a viscerally unsettling addition to the possession horror canon.

title: Bride of Frankenstein
rt_critics_consensus: An eccentric, campy, technically impressive, and frightening picture, James Whale's Bride of Frankenstein has aged remarkably well.

title: Doctor Jekyll
rt_critics_consensus: Doctor Jekyll is a meandering reinterpretation whose gothic ambitions can't overcome its hollow storytelling and awkward tonal choices.

title: Knives Out
rt_critics_consensus: Knives Out sharpens old murder-mystery tropes with a keenly assembled suspense outing that makes brilliant use of writer-director Rian Johnson's stellar ensemble.

title: Resident Evil
rt_critics_consensus: Like other video game adapations, Resident Evil is loud, violent, formulaic, and cheesy.

title: The Return of the Living Dead
rt_critics_consensus: The Return of the Living Dead is a gleefully grotesque horror-comedy, though its broad performances and cheap thrills sometimes detract from its sharper moments.

title: A Dark Song
rt_critics_consensus: A Dark Song offers atmospheric, unsettling horror -- and marks writer-director Liam Gavin as one to watch.

title: Moonstruck
rt_critics_consensus: Led by energetic performances from Nicolas Cage and Cher, Moonstruck is an exuberantly funny tribute to love and one of the decade's most appealing comedies.

title: Strange Encounters, Vampires, UFOs and Hauntings
Down a Dark Hall
rt_critics_consensus: Down a Dark Hall is more stylish than scary, although its foreboding atmosphere may raise a few goosebumps among younger viewers.

title: Murder in the Heartland: The Search for Video X
The Public Cemetery Under the Moon
Killer Raccoons 2: Dark Christmas in the Dark
Totally Killer
rt_critics_consensus: Totally Killer may not take full advantage of its promising conceit, but this time-traveling horror/sci-fi mashup is still enjoyable overall.

title: No Man's Land
rt_critics_consensus: Bleak and darkly humorous, No Man's Land vividly illustrates the absurdity of war.

title: There's No Such Thing as Vampires
Leviathan
rt_critics_consensus: A deep-sea thriller with an unusually strong cast and potent ideas, Leviathan quickly plunges into an abyss of weak thrills and lame kills.

title: Alone in the Dark
rt_critics_consensus: Inept on almost every level, Alone in the Dark may not work as a thriller, but it's good for some head-slapping, incredulous laughter.

title: A Fantastic Fear of Everything
rt_critics_consensus: Aside from a few chuckles and Simon Pegg's reliably affable screen presence, A Fantastic Fear of Everything has embarrassingly little to offer.

title: Starship Troopers 2: Hero of the Federation
She Came from the Woods
rt_critics_consensus: An uneasy '80s-set blend of comedy and horror, She Came from the Woods is just fast-paced and inventive enough to recommend.

title: Out Come the Wolves
rt_critics_consensus: Out Come the Wolves' bursts of terror and quiet tension keep it engaging even as its pared-down script struggles to build a truly satisfying narrative.

title: Anna and the Apocalypse
rt_critics_consensus: Anna and the Apocalypse finds fresh brains and a lot of heart in the crowded zombie genre - not to mention a fun genre mashup populated by rootable characters.

title: No Such Thing
rt_critics_consensus: Stocked with talented stars but lacking any clear idea of what to do with them, No Such Thing is ultimately far too uneven to recommend.

title: Children of the Corn IV: The Gathering
It Is Fine. Everything Is Fine!
The Condemned
rt_critics_consensus: The Condemned is a morally ambiguous, exceedingly violent and mostly forgettable action film.

title: The Shallows
rt_critics_consensus: Lean and solidly crafted, The Shallows transcends tired shark-attack tropes with nasty thrills and a powerful performance from Blake Lively.

title: War Games: At the End of the Day
Evil Dead II
rt_critics_consensus: Less a continuation than an outright reimagining, Sam Raimi transforms his horror tale into a comedy of terrors -- and arguably even improves on the original formula.

title: Deadstream
rt_critics_consensus: Proof that there's still life in the found-footage gimmick, Deadstream is a scarily good bit of B-movie fun.

title: Within the Woods of Undead County
Inland Empire
rt_critics_consensus: Typical David Lynch fare: fans of the director will find Inland Empire seductive and deep. All others will consider the heady surrealism impenetrable and pointless.

title: The Gateway
rt_critics_consensus: With a sturdy framework but too many unnecessary parts, The Gateway leads to a bit of a B-movie dead end.

title: The Dish
rt_critics_consensus: A feel good movie without an abundance of mush.

title: Ravenous
rt_critics_consensus: Ravenous tries bringing cannibal horror into an Old West setting, ending up with an uneven blend that will fail to satisfy most fans of either genre.

title: Riding the Bullet
rt_critics_consensus: Stephen King adaptation veteran director Mick Garris has lofty storytelling goals which ultimately flail and undercut the story's terror.

title: Doomsday
rt_critics_consensus: Doomsday is a pale imitation of previous futuristic thrillers, minus the cohesive narrative and charismatic leads.

title: Vacancy
rt_critics_consensus: Vacancy's restraint with gore is commendable, the thin characters and B-movie cliches less so.

title: What's Cooking?
rt_critics_consensus: What's Cooking? is well-acted, but the scenes sometimes sink into melodrama as characters scream at each other, and the movie as a whole is too lightweight and forgettable.

title: Horror in the High Desert 2: Minerva
Rumours
rt_critics_consensus: A brainy satire that has a ball turning the supposed adults in the room into helpless children, Rumours spins a one-joke premise into a sophisticated riot.

title: Infinite
rt_critics_consensus: An initially intriguing sci-fi thriller that quickly veers into incoherence, Infinite is as inane as it is inconsequential.

title: Martin
rt_critics_consensus: George A. Romero's contribution to vampire lore contains the expected gore and social satire -- but it's also surprisingly thoughtful, and boasts a whopper of a final act.

title: Slaxx
rt_critics_consensus: A slim-cut slice of bizarre horror, Slaxx effectively balances quirk with gore -- and has the good sense to get out before its oddball premise starts showing its seams.

title: At Midnight
rt_critics_consensus: Diego Boneta and Monica Barbaro are appealing, well-matched leads -- here's hoping they reunite for a picture better than the strained and predictable At Midnight.

title: Plaga zombie: zona mutante: revolución tóxica
Sarah, Plain and Tall: Winter's End
A Nightmare Wakes
rt_critics_consensus: A Nightmare Wakes revisits the creation of a classic story from an admirably novel perspective; unfortunately, it never quite lumbers to life.

title: Honeydew
rt_critics_consensus: Atmospheric horror built on layers of creeping dread, Honeydew finds fresh frights in its familiar setup -- and portends great things for writer-director Devereux Milburn.

title: Winter Ghost Stories - I and Me and Grandma's Story
Children of the Corn II: The Final Sacrifice
Matinee
rt_critics_consensus: Smart, funny, and disarmingly sweet, Matinee is a film that film buffs will love -- and might even convert some non-believers.

title: Abraham Lincoln: Vampire Hunter
rt_critics_consensus: Abraham Lincoln: Vampire Hunter has visual style to spare, but its overly serious tone doesn't jibe with its decidedly silly central premise, leaving filmgoers with an unfulfilling blend of clashing ingredients.

title: Final Destination 2
rt_critics_consensus: Final Destination 2 is little more than an excuse to stage elaborate, gory scenes of characters getting killed off.

title: Rats 2: They're Back With a Vengeance
Final Destination 5
rt_critics_consensus: It's still only for the gore-thirsty faithful, but Final Destination 5 represents a surprising return to form for the franchise.

title: Skin
rt_critics_consensus: Skin could stand to go a bit deeper below its surface, but a worthy story and a committed performance from Jamie Bell make this timely drama well worth a watch.

title: Video Diary of a Lost Girl
Saloum
rt_critics_consensus: Smart, dynamic, and fast-paced, Saloum mixes tones and genres into a tart, smoothly blended treat.

title: A Haunted House
rt_critics_consensus: A Haunted House seeks to offend every sensibility, but it's greatest sin: not being funny!

title: I Told You Not to Call the Police
The Night House
rt_critics_consensus: Led by Rebecca Hall's gripping central performance, The Night House offers atmospheric horror that engages intellectually as well as emotionally.

title: Tape
rt_critics_consensus: Tape's stagebound feel is balanced by the engrossing psychodrama of its storyline, which allows a committed cast to shine.

title: Errementari: The Blacksmith and the Devil
Party Monster
rt_critics_consensus: The lurid display of camp soon turns tedious.

title: Choose or Die
rt_critics_consensus: Flickers of frightening promise are muffled by an excessively franchise-focused story in Choose or Die, with frustratingly forgettable results.

title: Toys
rt_critics_consensus: Like a colorfully overengineered gewgaw on the shelf, Toys might look like fun, but its seemingly limitless possibilities lead mainly to confusion and disappointment.

title: Junk Bonds: The Return of Junkbucket
There's Someone Inside Your House
rt_critics_consensus: A likable cast and strong set pieces give There's Someone Inside Your House a lift, but they're outweighed by its messily misguided story.

title: Meatballs
rt_critics_consensus: Meatballs is a summer camp comedy with few surprises, but Bill Murray's riffing adds a spark that sets it apart from numerous subpar entries in a frequently uninspired genre.

title: The Transformations of the Transformations of the Drs. Jenkins
Hypochondriac
rt_critics_consensus: Hypochondriac mistakes a few common horror ailments for exotic afflictions, but this unsettling look at mental illness and trauma proves properly contagious.

title: Yes, I Can See Dead People

rt_critics_consensus: Roland Emmerich's 2012 provides plenty of visual thrills, but lacks a strong enough script to support its massive scope and inflated length.

title: The Conjuring 2
rt_critics_consensus: The Conjuring 2 can't help but lose a bit of its predecessor's chilly sting through familiarity, but what remains is still a superior ghost story told with spine-tingling skill.

title: The Closet
rt_critics_consensus: A pleasant comedy with a great cast.

title: Queen of Spades - Through the Looking Glass
Await Further Instructions
rt_critics_consensus: A yuletide nightmare full of familial angst and slithering scares, Await Further Instructions is a genre treat that pretty much any horror fan will want in their stocking.

title: Hopscotch
rt_critics_consensus: Boosted by a deftly underplayed performance from Walter Matthau, Hopscotch is a Cold War spy caper with comic bounce.

title: The Shack
rt_critics_consensus: The Shack's undeniably worthy message is ill-served by a script that confuses spiritual uplift with melodramatic clichés and heavy-handed sermonizing.

title: The Curse of King Tut's Tomb
Freaky
rt_critics_consensus: An entertaining slasher with a gender-bending, body-swapping twist, this horror-comedy juggles genres with Freaky fun results.

title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple
rt_critics_consensus: A direct continuation of 28 Years Later that ups the gore while deepening the dread, The Bone Temple is finely adorned by Nia DaCosta's unnerving direction as well as Ralph Fiennes and Jack O'Connell's inspired performances.

title: Piercing
rt_critics_consensus: Stylish with a sadistic streak, Piercing pairs gripping lead performances with a smartly macabre story that delivers unpredictable thrills.

title: After Life
rt_critics_consensus: After Life is an offbeat and tender exploration of memory, love, and life after death.

title: Evil Dead Rise
rt_critics_consensus: Offering just about everything longtime fans could hope for while still managing to carry the franchise forward, Evil Dead Rise is all kinds of groovy.

title: Memory Box
rt_critics_consensus: Vibrantly filmed and finely detailed, Memory Box is an engaging depiction of one woman's reckoning with her past.

title: Butt Boy
rt_critics_consensus: Its puerile premise invites low expectations, but viewers who sit down in search of boldly humorous provocations won't leave Butt Boy brokenhearted.

title: The Jack in the Box: Awakening
The Birth of Kitaro: The Mystery of GeGeGe
Eden Lake
rt_critics_consensus: A brutal and effective British hoodie-horror that, despite the clichés, stays on the right side of scary.

title: JeruZalem
rt_critics_consensus: Jeruzalem's setting adds an interesting twist to this found-footage horror outing, even if the end result is still somewhat pedestrian.

title: Elevation
rt_critics_consensus: Beautiful scenery and a solid star turn from Anthony Mackie raise Elevation up to an extent, but this sci-fi thriller is too derivative to reach the peak of its potential.

title: Wishmaster 3: Beyond the Gates of Hell
Troll 2
rt_critics_consensus: Oh my god.

title: The Moose Head Over the Mantel
In Fabric
rt_critics_consensus: In Fabric's gauzy giallo allure weaves a surreal spell, blending stylish horror and dark comedy to offer audiences a captivating treat.

title: Hot Spring Shark Attack
rt_critics_consensus: Hot Spring Shark Attack takes a bite out of its audience with an astute wack-fest that manages to float above its shortcomings.

title: Antlers
rt_critics_consensus: It struggles to find a successful balance between its genre and allegorical elements, but Antlers is sharp enough to recommend as a richly atmospheric creature feature.

title: Next
rt_critics_consensus: Numerous plot holes and poorly motivated characters prevent Next from being the thought-provoking sci-fi flick it could've been.

title: The Frighteners
rt_critics_consensus: Boasting top-notch special effects and exuberant direction from Peter Jackson, The Frighteners is visually striking but tonally uneven.

title: You Won't Be Alone
rt_critics_consensus: Although it may strike some as too artsy for its own good, You Won't Be Alone puts a thoughtfully fresh spin on familiar horror tropes.

title: The haunting of the Lady Jane
The Scary of Sixty-First
rt_critics_consensus: It's hard not to admire its often ungainly attempts to achieve its ambitions, but The Scary of Sixty-First is a horror provocation that disappoints as often as it thrills.

title: Things Will Be Different
rt_critics_consensus: A small film brimming with big ideas, Things Will Be Different effectively teases the brain while shredding the nerves.

title: Held
rt_critics_consensus: Its core idea is solid, but Held squanders most of its intriguingly nasty potential with lackluster overall execution.

title: Don't Move
rt_critics_consensus: Sergio Castellitto's latest film has a heavy dose of melodrama and is populated with unlikable characters.

title: Death Rider in the House of Vampires
Werewolves
rt_critics_consensus: An unapologetically schlocky horror flick, Werewolves more or less scratches the genre itch without ever becoming the howling good time it could've been.

title: The Devil Told Me What To Do
The Man With the X-Ray Eyes
rt_critics_consensus: By turns lurid and disturbing, The Man with the X-Ray Eyes is a compelling piece of sci-fi pulp and one of Roger Corman's most effective movies.

title: The Craft: Legacy
rt_critics_consensus: Although director Zoe Lister-Jones has forged a new path for the weirdos of today, The Craft: Legacy's spells may only enchant fans of the original.
31 delivers all the high-energy gore Rob Zombie fans have come to expect, but a lack of fresh ideas and likable characters mean only the already converted need apply.

title: One Missed Call
rt_critics_consensus: One of the weakest entries in the J-horror remake sweepstakes, One Missed Call is undone by bland performances and shopworn shocks.

title: Shark Encounters of the Third Kind
Bloodsport
rt_critics_consensus: This is where it all began for the Muscles from Brussels, but beyond Van Damme's athleticism, Bloodsport is a cliched, virtually plotless exercise in action movie recycling.

title: Arcadian
rt_critics_consensus: Led by a trio of strong performances, Arcadian blends family drama and post-apocalyptic horror to visceral, emotionally stirring effect.

title: Love in the Time of Monsters
Tales to Tell in the Dark
Dracula Has Risen From the Grave
Bring Me a Skin for Dancing In
It Came Upon a Midnight Clear
Monsters, Marriage and Murder in Manchvegas
The Vourdalak
rt_critics_consensus: A visually sumptuous gothic tale with intriguing subtext flowing through its veins, The Vourdalak is a memorably stylish debut for director Adrien Beau.

title: Blow Out
rt_critics_consensus: With a story inspired by Antonioni's Blow Up and a style informed by the high-gloss suspense of Hitchcock, DePalma's Blow Out is raw, politically informed, and littered with film references.

title: Gaslight
rt_critics_consensus: George Cukor's invigorated screen adaptation of the Angel Street play comes to life through Ingrid Bergman's suspenseful standoff against the uncannily evil Charles Boyer to thrilling yet melodramatic effect.

title: Matador
rt_critics_consensus: Intertwining murder and seduction, Pedro Almodóvar's Matador is a provocative thriller that will shock even the most adventurous moviegoers.

title: El Miedo no Anda en Burro
Hansel, Gretel, and the Sensual Witch
The Melon Heads: House of Crow
The Act of Seeing With One's Own Eyes
Mom's Got a Date With a Vampire
His House
rt_critics_consensus: Featuring genuine scares through every corridor, His House is a terrifying look at the specters of the refugee experience and a stunning feature debut for Remi Weekes.

title: Et mourir de plaisir (Blood and Roses)(To Die with Pleasure)
Queen of the Damned
rt_critics_consensus: A muddled and campy MTV-styled vampire movie with lots of eye candy and bad accents.

title: KMJS' Gabi Ng Lagim: The Movie
Run Sweetheart Run
rt_critics_consensus: Run Sweetheart Run's heavy-handedness blunts the effectiveness of its message, but this remains a wily, unpredictable thriller.

title: Dogman Territory: Werewolves in the Land Between the Lakes
The House on Laura Anne Dr.
Hatchet
rt_critics_consensus: The over-the-top gore, campy acting, and dim cinematography may be part of Hatchet's self-described old-school ethos, but irony alone can't sustain a horror film.

title: In the Room Where He Waits
Terrifier
rt_critics_consensus: All about the splashy kills, Terrifier artfully introduces the unpredictable massacrer mime Art, yet fails to carve an entertaining story worthy of his wrath.

title: Black Sunday
rt_critics_consensus: Mario Bava's official narrative debut is a witchy nightmare steeped in gothic splendor, shot in chiaroscuro black and white and punctuated with startling gore.

title: Slaughterhouse Rulez
rt_critics_consensus: An uneven blend of horror and comedy that fails to satisfy on either front, Slaughterhouse Rulez aims for B-movie fun but doesn't make the grade.

title: Elvira, Mistress of the Dark
rt_critics_consensus: A gothic, campy, raunchy comedy elevated by Cassandra Peterson's iconic persona yet driven off course by one-note jokes, this isn't the best — or worst — introduction to Elvira, Mistress of the Dark.

title: Bless the Child
rt_critics_consensus: Bless the Child squanders its talented cast on a plot that's more likely to inspire unintentional laughs than shivers.

title: The Coed and the Zombie Stoner
Land of the Dead
rt_critics_consensus: George A. Romero's latest entry in his much-vaunted Dead series is not as fresh as his genre-inventing original, Night of the Living Dead. But Land of the Dead does deliver on the gore and zombies-feasting-on-flesh action.

title: Resident Evil: Retribution
rt_critics_consensus: Resident Evil: Retribution offers everything one might reasonably expect from the fifth installment in a heavily action-dependent franchise -- which means very little beyond stylishly hollow CGI-enhanced set pieces.

title: Candyman: Farewell to the Flesh
rt_critics_consensus: Doubling down on gore while largely abandoning the subtext and wit that made the original worthwhile, Candyman: Farewell to the Flesh disappoints.

title: Wes Craven's New Nightmare
rt_critics_consensus: Wes Craven's New Nightmare adds an unexpectedly satisfying - not to mention intelligent - meta layer to a horror franchise that had long since lost its way.

title: Return to House on Haunted Hill
The Wild Man of the Navidad
Hostel
rt_critics_consensus: Featuring lots of guts and gore, Hostel is a wildly entertaining corpse-filled journey -- assuming one is entertained by corpses, guts, and gore, that is.

title: Temple Wood: A Quest for Freedom
Leave No Trace
rt_critics_consensus: Leave No Trace takes an effectively low-key approach to a potentially sensationalistic story -- and further benefits from brilliant work by Ben Foster and Thomasin McKenzie.

title: The Devil and Father Amorth
rt_critics_consensus: The Devil and Father Amorth sets out to interrogate age-old questions of faith, but fails to find enough compelling answers -- or reasons for viewers to watch.

title: Final Destination Bloodlines
rt_critics_consensus: Adding some surprising emotional layers onto the ghoulish bones of Final Destination's mythology, Bloodlines ingeniously executes grisly set pieces with precision and turns impending doom into outrageous fun.

title: Friday the 13th: The Final Chapter
rt_critics_consensus: As lumberingly single-minded as its homicidal star, Friday the 13th - The Final Chapter adds another rote entry to an increasingly labored franchise.

title: The Searchers
rt_critics_consensus: The Searchers is an epic John Wayne Western that introduces dark ambivalence to the genre that remains fashionable today.

title: So Close
rt_critics_consensus: This HK actioner oozes with style.

title: The Other Side of the Underneath
Bones and All
rt_critics_consensus: Although its subject matter may be hard to stomach, Bones and All proves a deeply romantic and thought-provoking treat.

title: Girl Slaves of Morgana Le Fay
Cry Baby
rt_critics_consensus: John Waters' musical ode to the teen rebel genre is infectious and gleefully camp, providing star Johnny Depp with the perfect vehicle in which to lampoon his pin-up image.

title: Jaws
rt_critics_consensus: Compelling, well-crafted storytelling and a judicious sense of terror ensure Steven Spielberg's Jaws has remained a benchmark in the art of delivering modern blockbuster thrills.

title: Rebecca
rt_critics_consensus: Ben Wheatley's Rebecca remake is ravishing to behold, but it never quite gets to the heart of the classic source material -- or truly justifies its own existence.

title: Brooklyn 45
rt_critics_consensus: Although its story can feel circuitous and a bit baggy, Brooklyn 45's character-driven period horror is elevated by some excellent performances.

title: Rana: The Legend of Shadow Lake
Raising Cain
rt_critics_consensus: Raising Cain doesn't rank with Brian De Palma's best work, but John Lithgow's spellbinding split-personality performance makes this thriller hard to dismiss.

title: Animal Kingdom
rt_critics_consensus: With confident pacing, a smart script, and a top-notch cast, Animal Kingdom represents the best the Australian film industry has to offer.

title: Tremors
rt_critics_consensus: An affectionate throwback to 1950s creature features, Tremors reinvigorates its genre tropes with a finely balanced combination of horror and humor.

title: Monster
rt_critics_consensus: Charlize Theron gives a searing, deglamorized performance as real life serial killer Aileen Wuornos in Monster, an intense, disquieting portrait of a profoundly damaged soul.

title: Sunrise
rt_critics_consensus: Boasting masterful cinematography to match its well-acted, wonderfully romantic storyline, Sunrise is perhaps the final -- and arguably definitive -- statement of the silent era.

title: The Ladykillers
rt_critics_consensus: Hanks' performance in the lead role is inspired, but this is a relatively minor offering from the Coen brothers.

title: In The Earth
rt_critics_consensus: In the Earth's bleak kaleidoscope of horror is a hallucinogenic meditation on the residual pandemic fears that haunt humanity.

title: The Ring
rt_critics_consensus: With little gore and a lot of creepy visuals, The Ring gets under your skin, thanks to director Gore Verbinski's haunting sense of atmosphere and an impassioned performance from Naomi Watts.

title: Severance
rt_critics_consensus: A twisted and bloody spoof on office life, Severance nicely balances comedy and nasty horror.

title: The Young and the Damned
rt_critics_consensus: Los Olvidados casts an unsparing eye on juvenile crime -- and the systemically flawed societies that allow it to flourish.

title: Founders Day
rt_critics_consensus: Founders Day brings a few clever bits to bear on its '80s-inspired slasher story, but its scattershot tone and an overreliance on horror clichés prevent it from leaving much of an impression.

title: At the Devil's Door
rt_critics_consensus: At the Devil's Door has no shortage of creepy style -- unfortunately, that isn't enough to distract from an uninspired story that never capitalizes on its potential.

title: The Invisible Man vs. the Human Fly
The Neon Demon
rt_critics_consensus: The Neon Demon is seductively stylish, but Nicolas Winding Refn's assured eye can't quite compensate for an underdeveloped plot and thinly written characters.

title: It's Only the End of the World
rt_critics_consensus: It's Only the End of the World is stocked with talent and boasts a story steeped in conflict, but the end result proves a disappointing misfire from writer-director Xavier Dolan.

title: Badland Hunters
rt_critics_consensus: Badland Hunters entertains with all-out action, even if the story's far less impressive than the fight scenes.

title: Rokuroku: The Promise of the Witch
One Cut of the Dead
rt_critics_consensus: Brainy and bloody in equal measure, One Cut of the Dead reanimates the moribund zombie genre with a refreshing blend of formal daring and clever satire.

title: Roar
rt_critics_consensus: Roar may not satisfy in terms of acting, storytelling, or overall production, but the real-life danger onscreen makes it difficult to turn away.

title: Sight
rt_critics_consensus: A faith-based film with a sweetly humanist grounding, Sight blinks at delivering much tonal nuance but delivers its true inspirational tale with moving conviction.

title: WolfCop
rt_critics_consensus: There's a new sheriff in town, and this one bites: Wolfcop packs a silly punch with just enough fun to rip the laughter right out of you.

title: The Pest
rt_critics_consensus: The Pest aims for zany comedy with social commentary, misses, and lands squarely at offensive misfire thanks to John Leguizamo's over-caffeinated performance and a script laden with stereotypes.

title: Freddy's Dead: The Final Nightmare
rt_critics_consensus: Reducing the once-terrifying Dream Reaper into a goofy caricature, this joyless climax will leave audiences hoping Freddy stays dead.

title: Safe
rt_critics_consensus: Safe's eerie social satire and somewhat sterile stylization is balanced by comedic undertones and an impressive, understated performance from Julianne Moore.

title: Night Swim
rt_critics_consensus: Despite a promising start and a handful of solid scares, Night Swim is undone by a premise that just isn't strong enough to support a feature-length film.

title: Frequency
rt_critics_consensus: A tight blend of surprises and suspense keeps audiences spellbound.

title: Hounds of Love
rt_critics_consensus: Smartly constructed and powerfully acted, Hounds of Love satisfies as a psychological thriller with a few nasty surprises -- and marks writer-director Ben Young as a promising talent.

title: The Hunger
rt_critics_consensus: Stylish yet hollow, The Hunger is a well-cast vampire thriller that mistakes erotic moments for a satisfying story.

title: Destroy All Neighbors
rt_critics_consensus: While it struggles with pacing and an indulgently chaotic narrative, Destroy All Neighbors charms with inventive special effects and infectious cast chemistry.

title: Gray Matters
rt_critics_consensus: Gray Matters tries emulating Hollywood screwball classics, but the rapid fire dialogue and witticisms comes off only as a contrived gimmick.

title: Sator
rt_critics_consensus: Supernatural horror of a refreshingly subtle vintage, Sator uses one family's fight against demonic possession to cast its own dark, stubborn spell.

title: The Infiltrators
rt_critics_consensus: The Infiltrators takes a unique stylistic approach to an important topic, leaving audiences with a viewing experience that demands -- and rewards -- attention.

title: Spooked: The Ghosts of Waverly Hills Sanatorium
Starve Acre
rt_critics_consensus: Matt Smith and Morfydd Clark are terrific as embattled parents in Starve Acre, an upsetting folk horror tale that locates the doom in domesticity.

title: The Haunted, the Possessed and the Damned
Jeepers Creepers
rt_critics_consensus: Jeepers Creepers has a promising start. Unfortunately, the tension and suspense quickly deflates into genre cliches as movie goes on.

title: The Father's Shadow (A Sombra do Pai)
Pinocchio
rt_critics_consensus: Roberto Benigni misfires wildly with this adaptation of Pinocchio, and the result is an unfunny, poorly-made, creepy vanity project.

title: Spontaneous
rt_critics_consensus: A dark teen comedy with an explosive twist, Spontaneous reaffirms Katherine Langford as a rising star -- and marks debuting director Brian Duffield as a filmmaker to watch.

title: Fame
rt_critics_consensus: Just because Fame is a well-acted musical doesn't mean it flinches against its surprisingly heavy topics.

title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
rt_critics_consensus: Its laudable deviation from series formula not withstanding, Halloween III: Season Of The Witch offers paltry thrills and dubious plotting.

title: Sorority Babes in the Slimeball Bowl-O-Rama 2
Death of a Unicorn
rt_critics_consensus: Receiving some sparkle from Paul Rudd and Jenna Ortega's father-daughter rapport, Death of a Unicorn's broad satire is a bit too on the horn but makes for an entertainingly splattery creature feature.

title: Little Big Boy: The Death Stalker Murders
Calvaire
rt_critics_consensus: Calvaire (The Ordeal) has a certain amount of grim potential, but loses effectiveness by too often mistaking disturbing gore for genuine horror.

title: A Quiet Place: Day One
rt_critics_consensus: Grounded in raw humanity by Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn, this sideways entry into A Quiet Place finds fresh notes of fright to play amid the silence.

title: Taxidermia
rt_critics_consensus: Surreal and visually striking, Taxidermia is, at times, graphic and difficult to watch, but creatively touches on disturbing subjects with imagination and wit.

title: Bodies Bodies Bodies
rt_critics_consensus: Impeccably cast and smartly written, Bodies Bodies Bodies is an uncommonly well-done whodunit.

title: Powertool Cheerleaders vs the Boyband of the Screeching Dead
Cujo
rt_critics_consensus: Cujo is artless work punctuated with moments of high canine gore and one wild Dee Wallace performance.

title: The Photographer: Inside the Mind of a Psycho
Antibirth
rt_critics_consensus: Antibirth's outstanding performances anchor its wilder flights of fancy, making this surreal horror outing from writer-director Danny Perez more than a garish curiosity.

title: All Cheerleaders Die
rt_critics_consensus: All Cheerleaders Die sets out to subvert horror tropes, but ends up falling victim to many of the same trashy cliches it's trying to mock.

title: The Running Man
rt_critics_consensus: Combining slick production values and a strong cast with predictable thrills and a surprisingly muted Schwarzenegger, The Running Man is an uneven albeit occasionally rousing sci-fi action satire.

title: Repulsion
rt_critics_consensus: Roman Polanski's first English film follows a schizophrenic woman's descent into madness, and makes the audience feel as claustrophobic as the character.

title: Pontypool
rt_critics_consensus: Witty and restrained but still taut and funny, this Pontypool is a different breed of low-budget zombie film.

title: Scary Movie
rt_critics_consensus: Scary Movie overloads on crudity and grossness to get its laughs.

title: I Don't Want to Be Born (The Baby)(It Lives Within Her)(Sharon's Baby)
The Descent
rt_critics_consensus: Deft direction and strong performances from its all-female cast guide The Descent, a riveting, claustrophobic horror film.

title: Invasion Earth: The Aliens Are Here!
First Blood
rt_critics_consensus: Much darker and more sensitive than the sequels it spawned, First Blood is a thrilling survival adventure that takes full advantage of Sylvester Stallone's acting skills.

title: The Exorcism of Saint Patrick (Fresh Hell Presents)
Deerskin
rt_critics_consensus: Led by a daring performance from Jean Dujardin, Deerskin finds writer-director Quentin Dupieux working in a more accessible -- yet still distinctive -- vein.

title: The Long Walk
rt_critics_consensus: The Long Walk may require patience from some viewers, but they'll be rewarded with a thoughtful and alluringly atmospheric ghost story.

title: The Perfect Host: A Southern Gothic Tale
The Ceremony Is About to Begin
Hobu Chandra Raja Gobu Chandra Mantri
The Black Dahlia
rt_critics_consensus: Though this ambitious noir crime-drama captures the atmosphere of its era, it suffers from subpar performances, a convoluted story, and the inevitable comparisons to other, more successful films of its genre.

title: Attack of the Giant Blurry Finger
The Wailing
rt_critics_consensus: The Wailing delivers an atmospheric, cleverly constructed mystery whose supernatural thrills more than justify its imposing length.

title: The Bare Wench Project 2: Scared Topless
DASHCAM
rt_critics_consensus: DASHCAM is visually and thematically provocative, although the film's grating protagonist undercuts its effectiveness.

title: At the Meeting With Joyous Death
The Voices
rt_critics_consensus: The Voices gives Ryan Reynolds an opportunity to deliver a highlight-reel performance -- and offers an off-kilter treat for fans of black comedies.

title: Another Yeti: A Love Story: Life on the Streets
Body at Brighton Rock
rt_critics_consensus: Body at Brighton Rock leaves some of its thrilling potential untapped, but the end result remains a solidly crafted outing that should entertain horror fans.

title: Dark Matter
rt_critics_consensus: The creaky plotting, inscrutable characters, and unconvincing ending make it difficult for audiences to connect with Dark Matter.

title: Bingo Hell
rt_critics_consensus: Although its card is far from full, Bingo Hell delivers moderately effective socially conscious horror while suggesting greater things for director Gigi Saul Guerrero.

title: The Strange World of Coffin Joe
Un Chien Andalou
rt_critics_consensus: A hugely influential masterpiece stuffed with iconic sequences, Un Chien Andalou has lost none of its power to enthrall - or unsettle.

title: Sorority Girls and the Creature From Hell
Significant Other
rt_critics_consensus: Some surprising touches and a generally thoughtful approach help elevate Significant Other above standard wilderness survival stories.

title: Two Lovers
rt_critics_consensus: Two Lovers is a complex, intriguing, richly-acted romantic drama.

title: Who Invited Them
rt_critics_consensus: It's admittedly uneven, but Who Invited Them benefits from writer-director Duncan Birmingham's playful approach to genre tropes.

title: Jessabelle
rt_critics_consensus: Jessabelle hints at a bright future for star Sarah Snook, but clouds her performance with a cliched -- and tasteless -- storyline.

title: I Know What You Did in English Class
Black Eyed Children: Let Me In
Sinister 2
rt_critics_consensus: Sinister 2 has a few ingredients that will be familiar to fans of the original; unfortunately, in this slapdash second installment, none of them are scary anymore.

title: Arachnophobia
rt_critics_consensus: Arachnophobia may not deliver genuine chills, but it's an affectionate, solidly built tribute to Hollywood's classic creature features.

title: The Advent Calendar
rt_critics_consensus: It may not be the most effective holiday horror entry, but The Advent Calendar has its share of nasty treats for genre fans.

title: 3 From Hell
rt_critics_consensus: 3 from Hell may be of interest to fans of the trilogy, but those not already on board with Rob Zombie's gory saga will find little to lure them in here.

title: The Bad Batch
rt_critics_consensus: The Bad Batch has its moments, but it's too thinly written and self-indulgent to justify its length or compensate for its slow narrative drift.

title: Handling the Undead
rt_critics_consensus: A grounded zombie picture with emotional bite, Handling the Undead shuffles along at a patient pace but succeeds as a poignant and thoughtful addition to the genre.

title: Contempt
rt_critics_consensus: This powerful work of essential cinema joins "meta" with "physique," casting Brigite Bardot and director Godard's inspiration Fritz Lang.

title: A Field in England
rt_critics_consensus: Recklessly assembled and occasionally compelling in spite of itself, A Field in England showcases a singularly brilliant voice in British cinema.

title: The Strangers: Chapter 1
rt_critics_consensus: Lacking the chilling suspense of the original The Strangers and proving to be just unpleasant, this Chapter 1 closes the book on itself.

title: Offseason
rt_critics_consensus: Led by strong performances from Jocelin Donahue and Melora Walters, Offseason is a solid supernatural horror story that casts a sneaky spell.

title: Queens of the Dead
rt_critics_consensus: Setting the stage to slay all day, Tina Romero's feature debut is a glowing zombie-fest filled with humor and heart, proving that there's more than enough room in the genre for these Queens of the Dead.

title: Silent Night, Deadly Night 3: Better Watch Out!
High School of the Dead: Drifters of the Dead
Dracula, Prince of Darkness
rt_critics_consensus: Never veering too far from the usual Hammer trappings, Dracula, Prince of Darkness casts an effectively vicious vampire yarn with its chilling atmosphere and spirited cast of characters.

title: Hellboy: The Crooked Man
rt_critics_consensus: Hellboy:The Crooked Man buckles under its constraints resulting in an uneven, third-rate bag of all tricks and no treats.

title: Jacob's Ladder
rt_critics_consensus: Even with its disorienting leaps of logic and structure, Jacob's Ladder is an engrossing, nerve-shattering experience.

title: Phantasm
rt_critics_consensus: Phantasm: Remastered adds visual clarity to the first installment in one of horror's most enduring -- and endearingly idiosyncratic -- franchises.

title: Motel Hell
rt_critics_consensus: Eerie and satirical, Motel Hell has no vacancy when it comes to low-brow horror gags.

title: Breach
rt_critics_consensus: Powered by Chris Cooper's masterful performance, Breach is a tense and engaging portrayal of the FBI's infamous turncoat.

title: I Know Who Killed Me
rt_critics_consensus: Distasteful and ludicrously plotted, I Know Who Killed Me is a career nadir for all involved -- particularly Lindsay Lohan in a dual role.

title: Vampire's Kiss
rt_critics_consensus: He's a vampire! He's a vampire! He's a vampire!

title: Witchcraft III: The Kiss of Death
The Deep End
rt_critics_consensus: A well-acted film noir with arresting visuals.

title: Gnaw: Food of the Gods II
The Man With the Synthetic Brain
Captivity
rt_critics_consensus: Lacking scares or psychological insight, Captivity is a distasteful entry in the 'torture porn' subgenre.

title: Hour of the Wolf
rt_critics_consensus: Ingmar Bergman makes a successful foray into horror with Hour of the Wolf, infusing the demons that spring from creativity with his trademark psychological curiosity.

title: Look What's Happened to Rosemary's Baby
The Hallow
rt_critics_consensus: Drenched in dark atmosphere and bolstered with some real emotion underneath all the chills, The Hallow suggests a wonderfully horrifying future for director Corin Hardy.

title: Children of the Corn
rt_critics_consensus: Bereft of a single kernel of fear or excitement, this Children of the Corn suggests the franchise is irrevocably lost in a maize of sub-mediocrity.

title: The Fortune Cookie
rt_critics_consensus: Uniting Jack Lemmon and a never-better Walter Matthau on the screen for the first time, The Fortune Cookie continues director Billy Wilder's winning streak of sweet comedies with a nice snap.

title: Warlock III: The End of Innocence
Raw 3: The Revelation of Grete Müller
The Housemaid
rt_critics_consensus: Im Sang-soo's remake of The Housemaid struggles to escape the shadow of the original, but offers its own unique -- and decidedly sensual -- pleasures.

title: Kill Time (Mou sha si shui nian hua)
I Blame Society
rt_critics_consensus: Spurts of dark humor gush out of I Blame Society's satirical approach to filmmaking and celebrity culture, prodded by sharp societal observations from writer-director-star Gillian Wallace Horvat.

title: The Best of Me
rt_critics_consensus: At nine films and counting, the line between Nicholas Sparks film fans and detractors is clear, and The Best of Me will change few minds on either side of the divide.

title: Trucker
rt_critics_consensus: Excellent performances by Michelle Monaghan and Nathan Fillion and keenly-observed details of small town life elevate this otherwise predictable and inconsistent melodrama.

title: Mute Witness
rt_critics_consensus: Mute Witness is a slickly crafted horror/thriller with some surprising comic twists.

title: Blood Orgy of the Leather Girls
The Lodgers
rt_critics_consensus: Like many real-life long-term visitors, The Lodgers starts out with a modicum of mystery and excitement, but eventually overstays its welcome.

title: The Tango of the Widower and Its Distorting Mirror
Shaun of the Dead
rt_critics_consensus: Shaun of the Dead cleverly balances scares and witty satire, making for a bloody good zombie movie with loads of wit.

title: What Happened to Dorothy Bell?
rt_critics_consensus: A subtle, psychologically driven found footage, What Happened to Dorothy Bell? rises above genre conventions with its haunting exploration of inherited trauma and hidden family pain.

title: I Spit on Your Corpse, I Piss on Your Grave
The Room
rt_critics_consensus: A bona fide classic of midnight cinema, Tommy Wiseau's misguided masterpiece subverts the rules of filmmaking with a boundless enthusiasm that renders such mundanities as acting, screenwriting, and cinematography utterly irrelevant. You will never see a football the same way again.

title: Stalked by My Doctor: Patient's Revenge
The Moth Diaries
rt_critics_consensus: Left in the dust of yesterday's vampiric novelties, The Moth Diaries swarms in the wind, amidst lackluster thrills and a script with too many holes to count.

title: From Hell to the Wild West
Sam and Colby: The Legends of the Paranormal
A Black Rift Begins to Yawn
From Dusk Till Dawn 2: Texas Blood Money
The Forgotten
rt_critics_consensus: The premise grows too ridiculous to take seriously.

title: Lifeboat
rt_critics_consensus: Hitchcock proves he can wring suspense from the most confined of settings aboard a raft teeming with vivid personalities in this maritime thriller.

title: As in Heaven, So on Earth
The Dark Side of the Moon
Black Christmas
rt_critics_consensus: A gratuitous remake of the 1974 slasher, Black Christmas pumps out the gore and blood with zero creativity, humor, or visual flair.

title: Identity Thief
rt_critics_consensus: Identity Thief's few laughs are attributable to Melissa McCarthy and Jason Bateman, who labor mightily to create a framework for the movie's undisciplined plotline.

title: Summertime
rt_critics_consensus: With Katharine Hepburn and the city of Venice glowing under David Lean's direction, Summertime is a swooning romance for the ages.

title: Dracula: Dead and Loving It
rt_critics_consensus: Lacking any of the comedic bite that audiences have come to expect from a Mel Brooks farce, this vampire parody just plain ol' sucks.

title: Abominable
rt_critics_consensus: Working with admittedly familiar ingredients, Abominable offers audiences a beautifully animated and overall engaging adventure that the whole family can enjoy.

title: Day Shift
rt_critics_consensus: Game stars and an appealingly goofy premise aren't enough to make up for Day Shift's uninspired action-comedy hijinks.

title: Bloody Trip: Rise of the Dead
Hell House
rt_critics_consensus: A fascinating, even-handed account of Hell Houses.

title: The Man from the Sea (Umi wo kakeru)
The 13th Child, Legend of the Jersey Devil
Babel
rt_critics_consensus: In Babel, there are no villains, only victims of fate and circumstance. Director Alejandro Gonzalez Iñarritu weaves four of their woeful stories into this mature and multidimensional film.

title: Soul
rt_critics_consensus: A film as beautiful to contemplate as it is to behold, Soul proves Pixar's power to deliver outstanding all-ages entertainment remains undimmed.

title: Most Beautiful Island
rt_critics_consensus: Most Beautiful Island plunges audiences into a little-seen sector of society, with writer-director Ana Asensio's fearless performance leading the way.

title: Are You in the House Alone?
A Cure for Wellness
rt_critics_consensus: A Cure for Wellness boasts a surfeit of visual style, but it's wasted on a derivative and predictable story whose twists, turns, and frights have all been more effectively dealt before.

title: Insidious
rt_critics_consensus: Aside from a shaky final act, Insidious is a very scary and very fun haunted house thrill ride.

title: Direct to Video: Straight to Video Horror of the 90s
Blood Lake: Attack of the Killer Lampreys
The Night Eats the World
rt_critics_consensus: The Night Eats the World finds a few unexplored corners in the crowded zombie genre, with a refreshing emphasis on atmosphere and character development.

title: The Night Beyond the Tricornered Window
Rotkäppchen: The Blood of Red Riding Hood
Ed Gein: The Butcher of Plainfield
You'll Never Find Me
rt_critics_consensus: Visually and sonically distinctive, You'll Never Find Me heightens its spooky atmosphere and claustrophobic setting with a story that'll keep viewers guessing.

title: Antiviral
rt_critics_consensus: Antiviral is well-crafted body horror, packed with interesting -- if not entirely subtle -- ideas.

title: Pumpkin
rt_critics_consensus: The messy Pumpkin wastes its premise by not making the satire sharp enough.

title: Idle Hands
rt_critics_consensus: An uneasy mix of slapstick and gore, Idle Hands lacks the manic energy and comedic inspiration required to pull off its goofy premise.

title: Dear John
rt_critics_consensus: Built from many of the same ingredients as other Nicholas Sparks tearjerkers, Dear John suffers from its cliched framework, as well as Lasse Hallstrom's curiously detached directing.

title: The Spirit
rt_critics_consensus: Though its visuals are unique, The Spirit's plot is almost incomprehensible, the dialogue is ludicrously mannered, and the characters are unmemorable.

title: Hunter Hunter
rt_critics_consensus: Bloody and brutal, Hunter Hunter is a hard-hitting survivalist thriller that ratchets its tension with precision.

title: Bloodsucking Bastards
rt_critics_consensus: Bloodsucking Bastards gets a few gallons of B-movie fun out of its sanguine humor and solid cast, even if it isn't quite as wild as its title might suggest.

title: From Hell
rt_critics_consensus: Visually impressive, but this latest Ripper tale is dull and far from scary.

title: VFW
rt_critics_consensus: VFW's solid cast, deft direction, and surprisingly weighty subtext add extra heft to a gory thriller that should hold buckets of sanguine appeal for grindhouse fans.

title: Bone Tomahawk
rt_critics_consensus: Bone Tomahawk's peculiar genre blend won't be for everyone, but its gripping performances and a slow-burning story should satisfy those in search of something different.

title: The Cured
rt_critics_consensus: The zombie apocalypse genre is crowded with clichés, but The Cured sets itself slightly apart with some extra BRAAAAAAAAAAINS and thematic depth.

title: Frankenstein and the Monster From Hell
Spirits of the Dead
rt_critics_consensus: Three auteurs descend on the works of Poe, each putting on a ghoulish show -- adapting The Tomahawk Man's tales of dreams and fright, with Fellini's segment particularly out of sight.

title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
rt_critics_consensus: A by-the-numbers slasher that arrived a decade too late, the mostly tedious I Know What You Did Last Summer will likely only hook diehard fans of the genre.

title: The Forest of the Lost Souls
Devoured: The Legend of Alfred Packer
Rabbit Hole
rt_critics_consensus: It's often painful to watch, but Rabbit Hole's finely written script and convincing performances make it worth the effort.

title: The Devil Comes to Kansas City
The Woman Who Keeps a Murderer
Girl on the Third Floor
rt_critics_consensus: Girl on the Third Floor proves a good old-fashioned haunted house story can still be entertaining -- and put a fresh spin on established genre formula.

title: Prime
rt_critics_consensus: Though Streep is dependably terrific in her role, the rest of the movie is too sitcom-ish, and the romance itself is dull.

title: Summer of 84
rt_critics_consensus: Summer of '84 suffers from an overreliance on nostalgia for its titular decade, but a number of effective jolts may still satisfy genre enthusiasts.

title: Underworld: Awakening
rt_critics_consensus: There's more vapid action and less story in Underworld Awakening than previous installments, making the whole affair feel inconsequential.

title: You Should Have Left
rt_critics_consensus: You Should Have Left hints at a genuinely creepy experience, but never quite manages to distill its intriguing ingredients into a consistently satisfying whole.

title: Death of Me
rt_critics_consensus: Despite a handful of decent jolts and Maggie Q's committed performance, Death of Me's intriguing premise is undone by its listless and largely scare-free execution.

title: Extra Ordinary
rt_critics_consensus: A horror/rom-com hybrid that somehow manages to blend its ingredients without losing their flavor, Extra Ordinary more than lives up to its title.

title: Big Daddy
rt_critics_consensus: Adam Sandler acquits himself admirably, but his charm isn't enough to make up for Big Daddy's jarring shifts between crude humor and mawkish sentimentality.

title: I Had a Bloody Good Time at House Harker
This Must Be the Place
rt_critics_consensus: This Must Be the Place may feature too many wayward detours to satisfy some passengers, but Sean Penn's beguiling performance and Paolo Sorrentino's scenic visuals make this a road trip worth following.

title: The Babadook
rt_critics_consensus: The Babadook relies on real horror rather than cheap jump scares -- and boasts a heartfelt, genuinely moving story to boot.

title: Cropsey
rt_critics_consensus: Riveting and bone-chillingly creepy, Cropsey manages to be one of the best documentaries and one of the best horror movies of the year.

title: The Other Lamb
rt_critics_consensus: Smartly directed by Malgorzata Szumowska, The Other Lamb uses a young woman's coming-of-age story to explore the powerful hold cults can have on their followers.

title: Revenge of the Empire of the Apes
Dangerous Animals
rt_critics_consensus: Stuffed to the gills with memorable shocks and a thrillingly unhinged heel turn by Jai Courtney, Dangerous Animals will be irresistible chum for horror aficionados.

title: Gazer
rt_critics_consensus: Hypnotic and stylish if a little too indulgent in its pacing, Gazer is an intriguing mood piece featuring a terrific performance by Ariella Mastroianni.

title: A Wounded Fawn
rt_critics_consensus: Delightfully dark and impressively ambitious, A Wounded Fawn offers a grimly distinctive treat for slasher fans.

title: Seven Stages to Achieve Eternal Bliss by Passing Through the Gateway Chosen by the Holy Storsh
The Platform 2
rt_critics_consensus: Grim and visually monotonous, The Platform 2 stands as an incoherent sequel that buries its compelling premise under dull symbolism, excessive gore, and a narrative that's hard to care about.

title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
rt_critics_consensus: Smart, solidly crafted, and palpably tense, 10 Cloverfield Lane makes the most of its confined setting and outstanding cast -- and suggests a new frontier for franchise filmmaking.

title: Werewolves Within
rt_critics_consensus: Werewolves Within is the rare horror comedy that offers equal helpings of either genre -- and adds up to a whole lot of fun in the bargain.

title: Outbreak
rt_critics_consensus: A frustratingly uneven all-star disaster drama, Outbreak ultimately proves only mildly contagious and leaves few lasting side effects.

title: Black Box
rt_critics_consensus: An intriguing debut for writer-director Emmanuel Osei-Kuffour, Black Box compensates for a lack of surprises with strong performances and an emotionally rewarding story.

title: MaXXXine
rt_critics_consensus: Intoxicating with its maxxximal style, MaXXXine is an uneven but vibrant pastiche that drives a stiletto through Hollywood's heart.

title: Hello Mary Lou: Prom Night II
The Deliverance
rt_critics_consensus: Lee Daniels' foray into horror works best when it leans into his melodramatic preoccupations, but it fails to deliver the exorcism goods.

title: Hallow Road
rt_critics_consensus: Minimalist in presentation while getting great mileage out of Rosamund Pike and Matthew Rhys' superb performances, Hallow Road is a nerve-wracking thriller that takes audiences down a haunting path.

title: A Guidebook to Killing Your Ex
Zombie Massacre 2: Reich of the Dead
Night of the Reaper
rt_critics_consensus: Night of the Reaper is an atmospheric indie slasher that balances genre enthusiasm and eerie direction to offer a fleeting but engaging diversion for dedicated horror fans.

title: Mad Heidi
rt_critics_consensus: Solid entertainment for viewers with twisted tastes, Mad Heidi is a midnight movie classic in waiting.

title: Philophobia: or the Fear of Falling in Love
Vampire in Brooklyn
rt_critics_consensus: Neither scary nor very funny, this misguided effort never lives up to its premise.

title: Happy Death Day 2U
rt_critics_consensus: A funnier follow-up with a sci-fi bent, Happy Death Day 2U isn't as fiendishly fresh as its predecessor, but fans of the original may still find this a sequel worth celebrating.

title: The Night of the Walking Dead
Shadowlands
rt_critics_consensus: Thanks to brilliant performances from Debra Winger and especially Anthony Hopkins, Shadowlands is a deeply moving portrait of British scholar C.S. Lewis's romance with American poet Joy Gresham.

title: Anything for Jackson
rt_critics_consensus: A talented cast and fiendishly frightening premise combine to make Anything for Jackson a must-watch for fans of supernatural horror.

title: The Last Lovecraft: Relic of Cthulhu
Children of the Corn III: Urban Harvest
Just Married
rt_critics_consensus: Just Married's plot is predictable, and the overdone pratfalls get tiresome.

title: Rashomon
rt_critics_consensus: One of legendary director Akira Kurosawa's most acclaimed films, Rashomon features an innovative narrative structure, brilliant acting, and a thoughtful exploration of reality versus perception.

title: The Cloverfield Paradox
rt_critics_consensus: Brilliant casting is overshadowed by a muddled mix of genres and storylines that scratch more heads than sci-fi itches in The Cloverfield Paradox.

title: The Oak Room
rt_critics_consensus: The Oak Room contains a taut, spooky tale that proves southern gothic can flourish in the snowy north.

title: Alien Resurrection
rt_critics_consensus: While Sigourney Weaver's feral performance as a resurrected Ripley restores some fun to the Alien franchise, the acid blood running through this fourth entry's veins corrodes whatever emotional investment audiences had left.

title: Masked Ghost Lady Presents: Scary Stories
Swamp Thing
rt_critics_consensus: Unabashedly campy -- often to its detriment -- Swamp Thing is not without its charms, among them Adrienne Barbeau as the damsel in distress.

title: Spawn
rt_critics_consensus: Spawn is an overbearing, over-violent film that adds little to the comic book adaptation genre.

title: Coming Home in the Dark
rt_critics_consensus: Smart, well-acted, and above all scary, Coming Home in the Dark finds first-time director James Ashcroft making his mark with a white-knuckle ride for horror fans.

title: Another Cabin in the Woods Movie
Serial Rabbit V: The Epic Hunt
The Cat o' Nine Tails
rt_critics_consensus: The Cat O'Nine Tails is a solidly entertaining Argento outing elevated by a well-chosen cast and the director's distinctive visual style.

title: Theater of Blood
rt_critics_consensus: Deliciously campy and wonderfully funny, Theater of Blood features Vincent Price at his melodramatic best.

title: Chain Reactions
rt_critics_consensus: Chain Reactions rewards horror fans and cinephiles alike with a thoughtful, unpretentious meditation on the cultural and artistic impact of one of cinema's most influential works.

title: I Drip Blood on Your Grave
Turn It Up
rt_critics_consensus: Reviewers say Turn It Up has a derivative feel, running through too many urban movie cliches.

title: Berlin Syndrome
rt_critics_consensus: Berlin Syndrome offers thriller fans an uncommonly well-written descent into dangerous obsession, enlivened by taut direction and a committed performance from Teresa Palmer.

title: We Are Still Here
rt_critics_consensus: Smart, powerfully acted, and devilishly clever, We Are Still Here offers some novel twists on familiar territory -- and heralds the arrival of a major talent in writer-director Ted Geoghegan.

title: V/H/S Halloween
rt_critics_consensus: Consistently unnerving as it cycles through a series of shorts that are more treat than trick, V/H/S Halloween is one of the most purely enjoyable cassettes in the franchise yet.

title: In the Eye of the Hurricane
Ilsa, She Wolf of the SS
Harvest of the Dead: Halloween Night
Ghostroads: A Japanese Rock N Roll Ghost Story
Opera
rt_critics_consensus: The Opera house location gives plenty to work with for director Dario Argento, who hits his decadently bloody high notes here.

title: Uchiage hanabi, shita kara miru ka? Yoko kara miru ka?
rt_critics_consensus: Fireworks seeks sparks in an ambitious blend of storytelling genres, but this misguided anime effort never truly takes flight.

title: The Ex
rt_critics_consensus: The Ex suffers from inept direction and characters that are either unsympathetic or plain unpleasant to watch.

title: Night of the Zoopocalypse
rt_critics_consensus: Night of the Zoopocalypse delightfully threads the needle as a child-friendly introduction to horror, delivering a gentle amount of good scares while dazzling the eye with its neon hues.

title: In the Folds of the Flesh
Bride of Re-Animator
rt_critics_consensus: Jeffrey Combs' Herbert West remains a wickedly devious presence, but Bride of Re-Animator can't revive the original's anarchically inventive spark, making it a fitfully entertaining but greatly diminished sequel.

title: The Lords of Salem
rt_critics_consensus: The Lords of Salem has lots of atmospheric portent, but it's unfortunately short on scares.

title: Saw VI
rt_critics_consensus: It won't earn the franchise many new fans, but Saw VI is a surprising step up for what has become an intricately grisly annual tradition.

title: Anacondas: The Hunt for the Blood Orchid
rt_critics_consensus: A cheesy monster B-movie.

title: The Twisted Death of a Lonely Madman
Proxy
rt_critics_consensus: Proxy's ambitious reach slightly exceeds its grasp, but its subversive narrative and alluringly dreamlike aesthetic make this horror mystery hard to ignore.

title: Sleight
rt_critics_consensus: Sleight subverts genre norms, delivering a smart and well-acted -- if ultimately somewhat frustrating -- magical mystery.

title: Before Night Falls
rt_critics_consensus: An impressionistic, fragmented look at Reinaldo Arenas, Before Night Falls' imagery manages to evoke a sense of the writer's artistry, and Bardem's strong performance holds the film together. Finally, a biopic done well.

title: I Spit on Your Grave: Deja Vu
Spirit Halloween: The Movie
rt_critics_consensus: Neither a trick nor much of a treat, Spirit Halloween: The Movie is a decent but overall unmemorable choice for families seeking spooky season viewing options.

title: The Conversation
rt_critics_consensus: This tense, paranoid thriller presents Francis Ford Coppola at his finest -- and makes some remarkably advanced arguments about technology's role in society that still resonate today.

title: Chopper
rt_critics_consensus: Eric Bana's performance as the charming but twisted Chopper is the highlight of this disturbing portrait about Australia's notorious author/criminal.

title: Quiet Nights of Blood and Pain
Sneakers
rt_critics_consensus: There isn't much to Sneakers' plot -- and that's more than made up for with the film's breezy panache and hi-tech lingo.

title: The Curse of the Living Corpse
The Most Wonderful Time of the Year
Armageddon
rt_critics_consensus: Lovely to look at but about as intelligent as the asteroid that serves as the movie's antagonist, Armageddon slickly sums up the cinematic legacies of producer Jerry Bruckheimer and director Michael Bay.

title: Cemetery Man
rt_critics_consensus: Cemetery Man will frustrate viewers seeking narrative cohesion or coherence, but this surreal blend of humor and horror should satisfy B-movie fans in the mood for quirk.

title: Cult of Chucky
rt_critics_consensus: Old Dolls can learn new tricks: This little murderer with a facelift is sillier and better than ever thanks to Don Mancini's Cult of Chucky.

title: Senritsu Kaiki File Kowasugi File 03: Legend of a Human-Eating Kappa
From Beyond
rt_critics_consensus: Though it sacrifices some depth in its characterizations, From Beyond stands as a stunningly grotesque Lovecraft adaptation with a dazzling blend of chilling effects and brainy, cosmic horror.

title: I will not take care of you.
Lord of Illusions
rt_critics_consensus: Lord of Illusions may come as something of a disappointment in the context of writer-director Clive Barker's best work, but genre fans should be reasonably diverted.

title: What Josiah Saw
rt_critics_consensus: What Josiah Saw may be too unrelentingly unpleasant for some viewers, but this slow-burning look at generational trauma leaves a lingering, nightmarish impact.

title: Sometimes They Come Back
rt_critics_consensus: Tim Matheson's gripping performance and a strong mood make Sometimes They Come Back an effective, if not wholly revelatory, Stephen King adaptation.

title: Godzilla, Mothra and King Ghidorah: Giant Monsters All-Out Attack
Assassination Nation
rt_critics_consensus: Assassination Nation juggles exploitation and socially aware elements with mixed results, but genre fans may find it too stylish and viscerally energetic to ignore.

title: Snakes on a Plane
rt_critics_consensus: Snakes on a Plane lives up to its title, featuring snakes on a plane. It isn't perfect, but then again, it doesn't need to be.

title: Enys Men
rt_critics_consensus: If its story's mysteries are ultimately less compelling than they might seem, Enys Men's retro aesthetic and intriguingly abstract visuals make this a chilly treat for horror fans.

title: The Hound of Hannibal :an American Werewolf
Destroyer
rt_critics_consensus: Destroyer's grueling narrative is as uncompromising as Nicole Kidman's central performance, which adds extra layers to a challenging film that leaves a lingering impact.

title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll & Mr. Hyde
Phantom of the Paradise
rt_critics_consensus: Brian De Palma's subversive streak is on full display in Phantom of the Paradise, an ebullient rock opera that rhapsodizes creativity when it isn't seething with disdain for the music industry.

title: What Have You Done to Solange?
Bloodthirsty
rt_critics_consensus: Offering a hauntingly lush style and plenty of gore, Bloodthirsty successfully captures the nightmarish darkness behind being hungry like the wolf in the quest for fame.

title: Seven
rt_critics_consensus: A brutal, relentlessly grimy shocker with taut performances, slick gore effects, and a haunting finale.

title: Limelight
rt_critics_consensus: A late triumph for one of early cinema's top talents, Limelight highlights the melancholy heart of Chaplin's comedic gifts.

title: Six Degrees of Separation
rt_critics_consensus: Though it betrays its theatrical roots, Six Degrees of Separation largely succeeds thanks to astute direction and fine performances -- particularly from an against-type Will Smith.

title: I Am the Pretty Thing That Lives in the House
Milk
rt_critics_consensus: Anchored by Sean Penn's powerhouse performance, Milk is a triumphant account of America's first openly gay man elected to public office.

title: Spiral: From the Book of Saw
rt_critics_consensus: Spiral: From the Book of Saw suggests an interesting new direction for the Saw franchise, even if the gory sum is rather less than its parts.

title: Regression
rt_critics_consensus: Regression boasts a pair of eminently likable leads -- neither of whom are able to dislodge the movie from the mire of psychological thriller mediocrity.

title: Nanny
rt_critics_consensus: Led by Anna Diop's strong central performance, the smartly disquieting Nanny is a promising debut for writer-director Nikyatu Jusu.

title: Cooties
rt_critics_consensus: A horror-comedy without enough of either, Cooties is fatally content to skate by on its intriguingly oddball premise.

title: In the Mouth of Madness
rt_critics_consensus: If it fails to make the most of its intriguing premise, In the Mouth of Madness remains a decent enough diversion for horror fans and John Carpenter completists.

title: Villains
rt_critics_consensus: Led by a quartet of strong performances, Villains offers genre fans a delightfully dark thriller with a sharp comic streak.

title: Girls With Balls
rt_critics_consensus: While occasionally funny and relentlessly gory, Girls With Balls is neither particularly strong on cheap thrills nor a very remarkable entry to the trash horror genre.

title: The Lazarus Effect
rt_critics_consensus: The Lazarus Effect has a talented cast and the glimmer of an interesting idea, but wastes it all on insipid characters and dull, recycled plot points.

title: The Abandoned
rt_critics_consensus: The Abandoned spends so much time setting up the creepy atmosphere, that it forgets to inhabit it with genuine spooks or scares.

title: Bird Box Barcelona
rt_critics_consensus: Expanding the franchise with some effective twists, Bird Box Barcelona lacks some of its predecessor's creepy tension but remains occasionally involving in its own right.

title: Idiot Girls and School Ghost: School Anniversary
The First Purge
rt_critics_consensus: The First Purge should satisfy fans of the franchise and filmgoers in the mood for violent vicarious thrills, even if its subtextual reach exceeds its grasp.

title: Rec 2
rt_critics_consensus: It lacks the surprising jolt of the first installment, but [REC] 2 almost maintains the original's chilling momentum -- and proves not all horror sequels were made equal.

title: Annabelle: Creation
rt_critics_consensus: Annabelle: Creation adds another strong chapter to the Conjuring franchise - and offers further proof that freaky-looking dolls remain reliably terrifying.

title: The Haunting of the Tower of London
Jack & Jill: The Hills of Hell
What We Do in the Shadows
rt_critics_consensus: Smarter, fresher, and funnier than a modern vampire movie has any right to be, What We Do in the Shadows is bloody good fun.

title: The Piano
rt_critics_consensus: Powered by Holly Hunter's main performance, The Piano is a truth-seeking romance played in the key of erotic passion.

title: Return of the Living Dead: Rave to the Grave
The Devil's Advocate
rt_critics_consensus: Though it is ultimately somewhat undone by its own lofty ambitions, The Devil's Advocate is a mostly effective blend of supernatural thrills and character exploration.

title: Hellbound: Hellraiser II
rt_critics_consensus: Hellbound: Hellraiser II retains the twisted visual thrill of its predecessor, although seams in the plot are already starting to show.

title: Fido
rt_critics_consensus: Making the most of its thin premise, Fido is an occasionally touching satire that provides big laughs and enough blood and guts to please gorehounds.

title: White Girl
rt_critics_consensus: White Girl isn't an easy watch, but it adroitly walks the line between exploitation and drama -- and marks an admirably assured debut for writer-director Elizabeth Wood.

title: Bela Lugosi Meets a Brooklyn Gorilla
The Exorcist: Believer
