rt_critics_consensus: The Exorcist: Believer earns points for trying to take the franchise back to its terrifying roots, but a lack of new ideas -- and scares -- make this an inauspicious start to a planned new trilogy.

title: The Purge
rt_critics_consensus: Half social allegory, half home-invasion thriller, The Purge attempts to make an intelligent point, but ultimately devolves into numbing violence and tired clichés.

title: Satanic Hispanics
rt_critics_consensus: A smooth blend of scares and dark humor, Satanic Hispanics is a well-crafted horror anthology whose occasionally jarring tonal shifts are easy to forgive.

title: Fear and Love: The Story of the Exorcist
Sissy
rt_critics_consensus: Sissy weaves timely themes into its rich blend of horror and dark humor, topped off by terrific work from a talented cast led by Aisha Dee.

title: The Program
rt_critics_consensus: Ben Foster's impressive efforts to channel Lance Armstrong are often enough to power The Program past director Stephen Frears' frustrating unwillingness to delve deeper into its real-life story.

title: The Dark Side of the Womb
The Changeover
rt_critics_consensus: The Changeover may not bite deep enough for more seasoned horror fans, but it's spooky enough for its younger target demographic.

title: Blood Work
rt_critics_consensus: Blood Work is a routine, but competently made thriller marred by lethargic pacing.

title: The Water Horse: Legend of the Deep
rt_critics_consensus: The Water Horse is a fine family film. It takes a classic tale and infuses it with extra imagination, sly humor, heart, and inventive special effects.

title: Firestarter
rt_critics_consensus: Firestarter's concept hews too closely to other known Stephen King adaptations, though it's got nice special effects (including scenery-chewing George C. Scott).

title: Pulse
rt_critics_consensus: Another stale American remake of a successful Japanese horror film, Pulse bypasses the emotional substance of the original and overcompensates with pumped-up visuals and every known horror cliche.

title: Warm Bodies
rt_critics_consensus: Warm Bodies offers a sweet, well-acted spin on a genre that all too often lives down to its brain-dead protagonists.

title: Feast
rt_critics_consensus: Director Gulager makes the most of what he's given; the resulting Feast offers up some surprisingly tasty -- if far from nourishing -- morsels.

title: Lords of Chaos
rt_critics_consensus: Lords of Chaos presents a grimly compelling dramatization of a real-life music scene whose aggressively nihilistic aesthetic spilled over into fatal acts of violence.

title: Seconds
rt_critics_consensus: Featuring dazzling, disorienting cinematography from the great James Wong Howe and a strong lead performance by Rock Hudson, Seconds is a compellingly paranoid take on the legend of Faust.

title: Bubba Ho-Tep
rt_critics_consensus: The best movie to star both the King and JFK.

title: Insidious: The Last Key
rt_critics_consensus: Insidious: The Last Key offers franchise star Lin Shaye another welcome opportunity to take the lead, but her efforts aren't enough to rescue this uninspired sequel.

title: The Devil Made Me Do It
Orphan: First Kill
rt_critics_consensus: Leaning into its ludicrous premise, Orphan: First Kill is a sequel that holds its own -- and for fans of campy horror, may even represent an improvement on the original.

title: Lonely Hearts
rt_critics_consensus: Several genres and plotlines intertwine in Lonely Hearts but don't connect, creating an uneven and unsatisfying film.

title: Coons! Night of the Bandits of the Night
Jaws 2
rt_critics_consensus: Jaws 2 never approaches the lingering thrills of its classic predecessor, but it's reasonably entertaining for a sequel that has no reason to exist.

title: The Haunting at Jack the Ripper's House
Better Watch Out
rt_critics_consensus: Carried by its charismatic young cast, Better Watch Out is an adorably sinister holiday horror film.

title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
The New Mutants
rt_critics_consensus: Rendering a list of potentially explosive ingredients mostly inert, The New Mutants is a franchise spinoff that's less than the sum of its super-powered parts.

title: Stephen King's Thinner
rt_critics_consensus: A bland, weightless horror film that seems to want to mock itself as the proceedings drag on.

title: Get Away
rt_critics_consensus: Drawing blood and yucks from its literal tourist trap setting, Get Away is uneven as comedy and horror but makes for a highly unique blending of the two genres.

title: Come True
rt_critics_consensus: Well-acted and visually striking, Come True offers an eerily effective reminder of how the sleeping subconscious can be fertile ground for horror.

title: In Dreams
rt_critics_consensus: Some interesting visuals, but the movie is as confusing as a dream.

title: The Orphanage
rt_critics_consensus: Deeply unnerving and surprisingly poignant, The Orphanage is an atmospheric, beautifully crafted haunted house horror film that earns scares with a minimum of blood.

title: Malum
rt_critics_consensus: Malum lures you to clock in for another Mason-esque Last Shift at a haunted police station with sheer blunt lore and gore.

title: Get Duked!
rt_critics_consensus: The rare comedy thriller that's funny as well as thrilling, Get Duked! marks writer-director Ninian Doff as a filmmaker with a bright future.

title: The Boy
rt_critics_consensus: The Boy could have gone in any number of scary or interesting directions, but instead settles for usual jump scares scattered throughout a pedestrian plot.

title: C.R.A.Z.Y.
rt_critics_consensus: Balancing heart and humor against outstanding work from a talented cast, C.R.A.Z.Y. proves the coming-of-age formula can still produce powerful results.

title: Sanatorium under the Sign of the Hourglass
The Strange Color of Your Body's Tears
rt_critics_consensus: Bursting with visual style but suffering from a dearth of discernible narrative, The Strange Color of Your Body's Tears is recommended only for giallo enthusiasts.

title: Vampires vs. The Bronx
rt_critics_consensus: In the sharp, socially conscious battle of Vampires vs. the Bronx, comedy and horror blend brilliantly -- and the audience is the winner.

title: Prom Night
rt_critics_consensus: A dim and predictable remake of an already dull slasher film, this Prom Night fails to be memorable.

title: The Curse of Blanchard Hill: Raped by Nature
Two of Us
rt_critics_consensus: A remarkable feature debut for director/co-writer Filippo Meneghetti, Two of Us tells a deceptively complex love story while presenting a rich acting showcase for its three leads.

title: Mutant Vampires From the Planet Neptune
The Harrisville Haunting: The Real Conjuring House
Venus
rt_critics_consensus: Audiences may attend to witness Peter O'Toole's Oscar-worthy performance, but they'll also be treated to a humane, tender exploration of maturing with both dignity and irreverence.

title: Shadow Zone: My Teacher Ate My Homework
Rent-a-Pal
rt_critics_consensus: Rent-A-Pal suffers from untapped thematic potential, but Wil Wheaton's unsettling performance makes this unique horror story worth watching.

title: The Tenants Downstairs (Lou xia de fang ke)
Smiley Face
rt_critics_consensus: Although many of the jokes have been done before, Anna Faris's bright performance and Gregg Araki's sharp direction make Smiley Face more than your average stoner comedy.

title: Shine
rt_critics_consensus: Featuring a strong performance from Geoffrey Rush, Shine succeeds in telling a compelling, inspirational story without resorting to cheap sentimentality.

title: King Kong
rt_critics_consensus: Featuring state-of-the-art special effects, terrific performances, and a majestic sense of spectacle, Peter Jackson's remake of King Kong is a potent epic that's faithful to the spirit of the 1933 original.

title: Death Note: Light Up the New World
The Dark and the Wicked
rt_critics_consensus: The Dark and the Wicked delivers on its title with an unsettling horror story whose deep dread and bleak outlook further compound its effective jolts.

title: House on Eden
rt_critics_consensus: A reliance on cheap jump scares and well-worn tropes leave House on Eden a forgettable and creatively exhausted found footage experience.

title: Clown in a Cornfield
rt_critics_consensus: Clown in a Cornfield doesn't reinvent the corn-maze, but its clever insights, subversion of expectations, and solid foundation help slash its way to the top.

title: Zom 100: Bucket List of the Dead
Cannibal Women in the Avocado Jungle of Death
Trick 'r Treat
rt_critics_consensus: A deftly crafted tribute to Halloween legends, Trick 'r' Treat hits all the genre marks with gusto and old fashioned suspense.

title: There's No Such Thing as Ghosts?
Resented Spirit of Baby Bride Groom
Jessie's Super Normal Regular Average Day
The Hunter's Anthology: The Demon Hunter
Molly
rt_critics_consensus: Molly never really elevates above uninspired, cliche-ridden moments.

title: Freddy vs. Jason
rt_critics_consensus: Fans of the two horror franchises will enjoy this showdown. But for everyone else, it's the same old slice and dice.

title: The Sixth Sense
rt_critics_consensus: M Night Shayamalan's The Sixth Sense is a twisty ghost story with all the style of a classical Hollywood picture, but all the chills of a modern horror flick.

title: Truth or Dare?: A Critical Madness
Silo Killer 2: The Wrath of Kyle
Good Manners
rt_critics_consensus: Good Manners adroitly juggles disparate tonal shifts while taking a uniquely smart and sensitive look at female relationships.

title: Ninth of September, the Cursed Day
Creep 2
rt_critics_consensus: Creep 2 has everything that made the original work and more -- more laughs, more awkwardness, more unsettling terror.

title: Snake Club: Revenge of the Snake Woman
I Know What You Did Last Christmas
Creepshow 2
rt_critics_consensus: Not even the melding of Stephen King and George A. Romero's writing sensibilities can elevate this spineless anthology, which is too simple in its storytelling and too skimpy on the genuine scares.

title: Gwen
rt_critics_consensus: Gwen's relentlessly grim outlook may wear on some viewers, but it remains a reasonably gripping, solidly assembled descent into atmospheric period horror.

title: The Haunted House: The Secret of the Cave
Apples
rt_critics_consensus: Apples explores human identity from a surreal and often humorous perspective, with peculiar yet ultimately thought-provoking results.

title: Oddity
rt_critics_consensus: An elegant and spooky ghost story punctuated with clever jolts, Oddity hews to the fundamentals of fright and achieves shout-inducing results.

title: Return to Cabin by the Lake
Sherlock Holmes Mare of the Night
See How They Run
rt_critics_consensus: See How They Run may not do itself many favors by asking for Christie comparisons, but this is still one fun whodunit.

title: Gimme Shelter
rt_critics_consensus: Equal parts essential and chilling, Gimme Shelter provides a spine-tingling look at how the Rolling Stones' music paralleled the end of the counterculture movement.

title: Jolly Roger: Massacre at Cutter's Cove
The Town That Dreaded Sundown
rt_critics_consensus: It may occasionally mistake more gore for genuine terror, but The Town That Dreaded Sundown is just stylish and clever enough to justify this second stab at the source material.

title: Mr. Wrong
rt_critics_consensus: A mean-spirited joke without a punchline, Mr. Wrong is so painfully unfunny that Ellen DeGeneres and Bill Pullman's lack of chemistry feels like a total drag despite being the point.

title: Half Moon
rt_critics_consensus: Bahman Ghobadi's Half Moon is a beautiful and often humorous look into the lives of Kurdish wanderers.

title: Here Comes the Devil
rt_critics_consensus: The power of Here Comes the Devil's atmospheric dread compels, but the plot and mystery are too muddled to leave a lasting impression.

title: There Is a Secret in My Soup
Summer Scars
rt_critics_consensus: This low budget British psycho-thriller packs plenty of atmospheric tension and suspense into it's slender running time.

title: I'm Dreaming of a White Doomsday
Wish Upon
rt_critics_consensus: Wish Upon is neither scary nor original, but its fundamental flaws as a horror movie may make it destination viewing for after-midnight camp genre enthusiasts.

title: The Clapper
rt_critics_consensus: The Clapper is tugged along by the valiant efforts of a talented cast, but it's ultimately not enough to make this wan romantic comedy worth a watch.

title: He's a Woman, She's a Man
Shocker
rt_critics_consensus: With an intriguing enough premise and horror legend Wes Craven on writer-director duty, the real Shocker here is just how lame the end results turned out to be.

title: OMG... We're in a Horror Movie
Easter Bunny Massacre: The Bloody Trail
Black as Night
rt_critics_consensus: It may not be particularly scary, but Black as Night has just enough bite to satisfy audiences in the mood for dark, teen-focused supernatural drama.

title: Stay
rt_critics_consensus: A muddled brain-teaser, Stay has a solid cast and innovative visuals but little beneath the surface.

title: The Front Room
rt_critics_consensus: The Front Room gets some mileage out of Kathryn Hunter's memorably grotesque turn as an in-law from hell, but the scatological scares in this family squabble are more off-putting than frightening.

title: La morte negli occhi del gatto (Seven Deaths in the Cat's Eye)
Revelation Road: The Beginning of the End
VHS Massacre: Cult Films and the Decline of Physical Media
Longlegs
rt_critics_consensus: Saturated in disquieting mood while leveraging a nightmarishly gonzo performance by Nicolas Cage, Longlegs is a satanic horror that effectively instills panic.

title: Earwig
rt_critics_consensus: Perplexing, unsettling, and alluring in nearly equal measure, Earwig may be as difficult to understand as it is to forget.

title: The Creature of the Sunny Side Up Trailer Park
In a Lonely Place
rt_critics_consensus: Led by extraordinary performances from Humphrey Bogart and Gloria Grahame, In a Lonely Place is a gripping noir of uncommon depth and maturity.

title: Panic Room
rt_critics_consensus: Elevated by David Fincher's directorial talent and Jodie Foster's performance, Panic Room is a well-crafted, above-average thriller.

title: John and the Hole
rt_critics_consensus: John and the Hole's enticingly unique premise and disquieting atmosphere are often at odds with a meandering story that prevents this thriller from fulfilling its creepy potential.

title: Suitable Flesh
rt_critics_consensus: Campy and creepy, Suitable Flesh delivers visceral, '80s-inspired horror topped off by game performances from Barbara Crampton and Heather Graham.

title: Luz
rt_critics_consensus: Luz takes a refreshingly unique approach to horror possession tropes, elevated by a chilly mood and minimalist scares.

title: Maverick
rt_critics_consensus: It isn't terribly deep, but it's witty and undeniably charming, and the cast is obviously having fun.

title: Friday Night
rt_critics_consensus: A dreamy, visually poetic film.

title: Waiting ...
rt_critics_consensus: Waiting... is a gross-out comedy that is more gross than comic.

title: Species
rt_critics_consensus: Species shows flashes of the potential to blend exploitation and sci-fi horror in ingenious ways, but is ultimately mainly interested in flashing star Natasha Henstridge's skin.

title: Vincent Must Die
rt_critics_consensus: Vincent Must Die's slow turn from absurd comedy to tense, horror-tinged drama is supported by smart storytelling and compelling work from Karim Leklou in the lead role.

title: Dr. Giggles
rt_critics_consensus: Larry Drake's deranged performance as the titular doctor is just about all that distinguishes Dr. Giggles from its slasher brethren.

title: A Haunting on Hamilton Street 2: The Stable
Cold Skin
rt_critics_consensus: Cold Skin exerts a strong visual pull, even if all that icy atmosphere isn't a truly satisfying substitute for a story that dares to go somewhere different.

title: Interview With the Vampire
rt_critics_consensus: Despite lacking some of the book's subtler shadings, and suffering from some clumsy casting, Interview with a Vampire benefits from Neil Jordan's atmospheric direction and a surfeit of gothic thrills.

title: Buddy BeBop vs the Living Dead
Silent Night, Bloody Night: The Homecoming
Lisa Frankenstein
rt_critics_consensus: An affectionate callback to classic horror comedies of the '80s, Lisa Frankenstein can be fun in its own right despite not quite measuring up to the movies it imitates.

title: Oliver!
rt_critics_consensus: Oliver! transforms Charles Dickens' muckraking novel into a jaunty musical Victorian fairytale, buoyed by Ron Moody's charming star turn and Onna White's rapturous choreography.

title: Saw X
rt_critics_consensus: Led by a franchise-best performance from Tobin Bell, Saw X reinvigorates the series with an installment that has a surprising amount of heart to go with all the gore.

title: Knife + Heart
rt_critics_consensus: Knife + Heart wrings giallo-inspired thrills out of a boldly challenging story that defiantly succeeds on its own stylish merits.

title: Gun Shy
rt_critics_consensus: A dark comedy of the low brow nature -- filled with fart and gay jokes. Even Liam Neeson and Sandra Bullock cannot save this failure.

title: Asylum: Twisted Horror and Fantasy Tales
Night Teeth
rt_critics_consensus: Night Teeth has a solid cast and some interesting ideas, but they're all lost in a listlessly told, generally predictable vampire story.

title: Imaginary
rt_critics_consensus: Imaginary's core concept is solid enough to produce a handful of scares, but too much of its potential is lost in a clichéd story that gets bogged down in world-building.

title: Poltergeist II
rt_critics_consensus: They're back, but this hollow sequel retains none of the charm or suspense that made the original Poltergeist such a haunting specter.

title: Singapore sling: O anthropos pou agapise ena ptoma
Paranormal Activity 3
rt_critics_consensus: While the jolts and thrills are undeniably subject to the diminishing returns that plague most horror sequels, Paranormal Activity 3 is a surprisingly spine-tingling treat.

title: Sayonara
rt_critics_consensus: As sumptuously staged as it is ponderously paced, Sayonara flutters by on the strength of its touching story and solidly assembled cast.

title: If I Was Your Girl Again
Ginger Snaps
rt_critics_consensus: The strong female cast and biting satire of teenage life makes Ginger Snaps far more memorable than your average werewolf movie -- or teen flick.

title: Turistas
rt_critics_consensus: Beautiful scenery and cinematography can't save Turistas from its wooden acting and stale and predictable plot.

title: It Comes at Night
rt_critics_consensus: It Comes at Night makes lethally effective use of its bare-bones trappings while proving once again that what's left unseen can be just as horrifying as anything on the screen.

title: Ted Bundy
rt_critics_consensus: Ted Bundy wastes an impressive performance from Michael Reilly Rourke on an exploitative film devoid of any social context or depth.

title: The Cave
rt_critics_consensus: Despite its stylized and impressive sets, this horror-monster movie mish-mash suffers from endless cliches and wildly implausible plotlines.

title: Wes Craven Presents: Dracula 2000
rt_critics_consensus: This retelling trys to offer a different spin on the origin of Dracula. Unfortunately, there's nothing here audiences haven't seen before.

title: Honey Bunch
rt_critics_consensus: An inventive horror flick with provocative ideas about fidelity to go along with its unnerving frights, Honey Bunch remains thrillingly unpredictable to the very end.

title: The Cabin in the Woods
rt_critics_consensus: The Cabin in the Woods is an astonishing meta-feat, capable of being funny, strange, and scary -- frequently all at the same time.

title: Raw: The Curse of Grete Müller
Aragami: The Raging God of Battle
The Nun II
rt_critics_consensus: Scarier than its predecessor, The Nun II makes for an entertaining addition to the Conjuring franchise despite not being the most original horror sequel.

title: Nope
rt_critics_consensus: Admirable for its originality and ambition even when its reach exceeds its grasp, Nope adds Spielbergian spectacle to Jordan Peele's growing arsenal.

title: A Christmas Horror Story
rt_critics_consensus: If you're in the mood for a fun enough genre film that kills its holiday theme, look no further than the surprisingly astute A Christmas Horror Story.

title: Cronicas
rt_critics_consensus: An unsettling and absorbing cautionary tale with John Leguizamo playing an unscrupulous TV reporter who uses the medium to further his own goals.

title: Dogtooth
rt_critics_consensus: It'll be too disturbing -- and meandering -- for some, but Dogtooth is as disturbing and startlingly original as modern filmmaking gets.

title: Grave Secrets: The Legacy of Hilltop Drive
Vampire Men of the Lost Planet
There's Something Wrong with the Children
rt_critics_consensus: Conceptually strong but uneven in execution, There's Something Wrong with the Children is a serviceable horror movie that could have been much more.

title: It Follows
rt_critics_consensus: Smart, original, and above all terrifying, It Follows is the rare modern horror film that works on multiple levels -- and leaves a lingering sting.

title: The Girl Who Killed Her Parents - The Confession
The Blackcoat's Daughter
rt_critics_consensus: Slow-building and atmospheric, The Blackcoat's Daughter resists girls-in-peril clichés in a supernatural thriller that serves as a strong calling card for debuting writer-director Oz Perkins.

title: Winnie the Pooh: Blood and Honey II
rt_critics_consensus: Winnie-the-Pooh: Blood and Honey 2 represents an improvement over the original in most respects, although the Poohniverse remains a place made for hardcore slasher fans.

title: The Good Night
rt_critics_consensus: That The Good Night is the work of a first-time writer/director is apparent, as the story feels disjointed and never connects with the audience.

title: Rising Boas in a Girl's School
Eraser
rt_critics_consensus: Eraser's shoot-'em-up action might show off some cutting edge weaponry, but its rote story is embarrassingly obsolete.

title: Before I Wake
rt_critics_consensus: Beware this waking nightmare: Before I Wake isn't always lucid enough to escape its wobbly story, but crafts a neat horror landscape of grief.

title: Senritsu Kaiki File Kowasugi File 01: Operation Capture the Slit-Mouthed Woman
Hannibal Rising
rt_critics_consensus: Hannibal Rising reduces the horror icon to a collection of dime-store psychological traits.

title: No One Will Save You
rt_critics_consensus: A home invasion thriller with an extraterrestrial twist, No One Will Save You serves up more genre fun from writer-director Brian Duffield -- and proves Kaitlyn Dever doesn't need much dialogue to command the screen.

title: Senritsu Kaiki File Kowasugi File 02: Shivering Ghost
The Devil's Candy
rt_critics_consensus: The Devil's Candy playfully subverts horror tropes while serving up more than enough stylish thrills to satisfy genre enthusiasts.

title: The Loneliest Boy in the World
My Inner Demon: A Geraldson Tale
The Cleanse
rt_critics_consensus: The Cleanse uses its unconventional premise as the framework for a suitably eerie and solidly well-acted horror outing with some surprising emotional heft.

title: Dark Prince: The True Story of Dracula
Drag Me to Hell
rt_critics_consensus: Sam Raimi returns to top form with Drag Me to Hell, a frightening, hilarious, delightfully campy thrill ride.

title: Cinderella
rt_critics_consensus: The rich colors, sweet songs, adorable mice and endearing (if suffering) heroine make Cinderella a nostalgically lovely charmer.

title: Home Sweet Hell
rt_critics_consensus: Home Sweet Hell mistakes misogyny for subversive wit, aiming for black comedy but ending up with a grueling test of viewer endurance that wastes game efforts from its likable leads.

title: OBEX
rt_critics_consensus: A beguiling odyssey that offsets its scrappy production values with sumptuously analog presentation, OBEX is a wildly inventive riff on the digital world.

title: The Silent House
rt_critics_consensus: Shot in a single take, The Silent House may be a gimmick movie, but it's one that's enough to sustain dread and tension throughout.

title: Crimson Peak
rt_critics_consensus: Crimson Peak offers an engaging -- albeit somewhat slight -- diversion driven by a delightfully creepy atmosphere and director Guillermo del Toro's brilliant knack for unforgettable visuals.

title: The Whistleblower
rt_critics_consensus: Rachel Weisz puts on a compelling smoldering act though the film suffers from a literal-minded approach to the material.

title: Basket Case
rt_critics_consensus: While Basket Case definitely delivers all the gonzo gore promised by its cracked premise, it's really set apart by its rich vein of genuine pathos.

title: Halloween: Resurrection
rt_critics_consensus: The only thing this tired slasher flick may resurrect is nostalgia for when the genre was still fresh and scary.

title: Psycho Ape: Part II - The Wrath of Kong
Anaconda
rt_critics_consensus: Anaconda's pulpy pleasures are constricted by its own absurdity, but creature feature fans may enjoy its brazen silliness.

title: Climate of the Hunter
rt_critics_consensus: Strong direction and an arresting sense of visual style make the vampire love triangle Climate of the Hunter a balmy treat for fans of off-kilter horror.

title: Bloodbath in the House of Knives
Control - Love Will Tear Us Apart
rt_critics_consensus: Control is a work of art, thanks to its evocative black and white cinematography and sensational performances from Sam Riley and Samantha Morton. Even those not familiar with Joy Division can still appreciate the beauty of the film.

title: The Strangers: Prey at Night
rt_critics_consensus: The Strangers: Prey at Night may appeal to fans of the original who've been jonesing for a sequel, but its thin story and ironic embrace of genre tropes add up to a bloody step back.

title: The Woman in Black 2: Angel of Death
rt_critics_consensus: The Woman in Black 2: Angel of Death is atmospheric and visually sharp, but it's short on tension and scares.

title: Halloween 4: The Return of Michael Myers
rt_critics_consensus: Halloween 4: The Return Of Michaels Myers may bring the series' masked killer back into the fold, but fails to offer the visceral scares and inventiveness of the original.

title: The Boy Behind the Door
rt_critics_consensus: A tense, terrifying, and all-around outstanding feature debut for its co-directing duo, The Boy Behind the Door should thrill discerning horror fans.

title: It's Alive III: Island of the Alive
Agora
rt_critics_consensus: Noble goals and a gripping performance from Rachel Weisz can't save Agora from its muddled script, uneven acting, and choppy editing.

title: If Footmen Tire You, What Will Horses Do?
Exorcism: The Possession of Gail Bowers
Strike, Dear Mistress, and Cure His Heart
Sorority Row
rt_critics_consensus: Though it's slick and stylish, Sorority Row offers nothing new to the slasher genre and misses the mark both in its attempts at humor and thrills.

title: Carol
rt_critics_consensus: Shaped by Todd Haynes' deft direction and powered by a strong cast led by Cate Blanchett and Rooney Mara, Carol lives up to its groundbreaking source material.

title: Mark of the Devil, Part II
Gozu
rt_critics_consensus: Miike continues his run of compellingly bizarre flicks.

title: Witch House: The Legend of Petronel Haxley
Milo
rt_critics_consensus: Bad Milo! sets some deliriously low expectations with its gross premise -- and then manages to match most of them in sick, entertaining style.

title: In a Violent Nature
rt_critics_consensus: As single-mindedly focused on mayhem as its demonic monster, In a Violent Nature serves up a deliciously disgusting feast for gore aficionados.

title: Bound
rt_critics_consensus: Bound's more titillating elements attracted attention, but it's the stylish direction, solid performances, and entertaining neo-noir caper plot that make it worth a watch.

title: A Banquet
rt_critics_consensus: If it isn't without its share of empty calories, A Banquet remains a lavishly appointed treat for fans of slow-burning horror.

title: Hell House LLC III: Lake of Fire
Christine
rt_critics_consensus: John Carpenter revs up Christine into a brisk thrill ride whose sharp direction, lively performances, and stylish flourishes make its absurd premise easy to forgive.

title: We Summon the Darkness
rt_critics_consensus: We Summon the Darkness makes the most of its rather pedestrian plot with palpable affection for genre formula, an appealing cast, and a sharp sense of humor.

title: The Possession
rt_critics_consensus: It may be based on a true story, but that doesn't excuse the way The Possession repeatedly falls back on hoary ghost movie clichés -- or the uninentional laughs it provides.

title: African American Psycho: The Hip Hop Freak
Always Shine
rt_critics_consensus: The tense and well-acted Always Shine tells an absorbing story while making a sharp, thought-provoking statement on women's roles in Hollywood.

title: Witchcraft V: Dance With the Devil
Flatliners
rt_critics_consensus: While it boasts an impressive cast, striking visuals, and an effective mood, Flatliners never quite jolts its story to life.

title: The Pale Door
rt_critics_consensus: Beyond The Pale Door lies an intriguing western/horror hybrid -- albeit one that struggles to fashion its halves into a compelling whole.

title: The Good People of Orphan Ridge
The Weapon, The Hour, The Motive
Caesar and Otto's Summer Camp Massacre
Repo! The Genetic Opera
rt_critics_consensus: Bombastic and intentionally gross, Repo! The Genetic Opera has a unique style but lacks the wit and substance to be involving.

title: Gilda
rt_critics_consensus: Rita Hayworth carries Gilda on the sheer strength of her screen presence, rendering the film's somewhat middling story almost irrelevant.

title: Children of the Corn 666: Isaac's Return
An Evening With My Comatose Mother
Annihilation
rt_critics_consensus: Annihilation backs up its sci-fi visual wonders and visceral genre thrills with an impressively ambitious -- and surprisingly strange -- exploration of challenging themes that should leave audiences pondering long after the end credits roll.

title: The Love Witch
rt_critics_consensus: The Love Witch offers an absorbing visual homage to a bygone era, arranged subtly in service of a thought-provoking meditation on the battle of the sexes.

title: Dybbuk Box: True Story of Chris Chambers
The Inerasable (Zan'e: Sunde wa ikenai heya)
The Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Beginning
rt_critics_consensus: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre: The Beginning is full of blood and gore, but not enough scares or a coherent story to make for a successful horror film.

title: The Outwaters
rt_critics_consensus: The Outwaters may strike some viewers as frustratingly withholding, but it remains an ambitious -- and overall effective -- slice of found-footage horror.

title: A Haunting on Dice Road 2: Town of the Dead
Barun Rai and the House on the Cliff
Sting
rt_critics_consensus: Sting should satisfy horror fans in the mood for a creature feature, even if the end results likely won't linger in the memory.

title: Desperate Measures
rt_critics_consensus: Times aren't desperate enough for this lame thriller to be called for.

title: False Positive
rt_critics_consensus: Its classic horror aims exceed its blood-slicked grasp, but False Positive works its way sneakily under the skin.

title: Leviathan: The Story of Hellraiser and Hellbound: Hellraiser II
Chernobyl Diaries
rt_critics_consensus: Despite an interesting premise and spooky atmospherics, Chernobyl Diaries is mostly short on suspense and originality.

title: Godzilla Minus One
rt_critics_consensus: With engaging human stories anchoring the action, Godzilla Minus One is one kaiju movie that remains truly compelling between the scenes of mass destruction.

title: Together
rt_critics_consensus: Though melodramatic at times, Together is heartwarming and well-acted.

title: Hitch
rt_critics_consensus: Despite Hitch's predictability, Will Smith and Kevin James win praise for their solid, warmhearted performances.

title: An Evening of Edgar Allan Poe
Blackthorn
rt_critics_consensus: Blackthorn invites comparisons to a classic Western -- and survives, thanks largely to a charismatic performance by a well-chosen Sam Shepard.

title: Killer Klowns From Outer Space
rt_critics_consensus: Killer Klowns from Outer Space's title promises darkly goofy fun -- and more often than not, the movie delivers.

title: Alien
rt_critics_consensus: A modern classic, Alien blends science fiction, horror and bleak poetry into a seamless whole.

title: In Flames
rt_critics_consensus: Conjuring fear with its sense of encroaching unease instead of cathartic jolts, In Flames is an exemplary horror film with something profound to say about the oppression of women.

title: Rabbit Trap
rt_critics_consensus: A bold creative swing that doesn't quite connect, Rabbit Trap captures the attention with its hypnotic soundscape but can't maintain its hold due to a disappointingly thin story.

title: Little Otik
rt_critics_consensus: Though rather overlong, Little Otik is a whimsical, bizarre treat.

title: Jeepers Creepers 2
rt_critics_consensus: Jeepers Creepers 2 is competently made, but it doesn't have the scares of the original.

title: The Balconettes
rt_critics_consensus: A Gallic farce that wildly veers between tones with a feminist undercurrent throughout, The Balconettes marks a promising creative progression for director-and-star Noémie Merlant.

title: 47 Meters Down
rt_critics_consensus: 47 Meters Down doesn't take its terrifying premise quite as far as it should, but its toothy antagonists still offer a few thrills for less demanding genre enthusiasts.

title: House of Spoils
rt_critics_consensus: Whipping up promising ingredients and elegantly plating them, House of Spoils has the presentation down but ultimately delivers empty calories.

title: Impetigore
rt_critics_consensus: Impetigore uses its folk horror setting as the brutally effective backdrop for a supernatural story that sinks its hooks into the viewer and refuses to let go.

title: Something In The Dirt
rt_critics_consensus: Something in the Dirt reaffirms Justin Benson and Aaron Moorhead as sci-fi storytellers with a distinctive -- and darkly alluring -- vision.

title: Annabelle
rt_critics_consensus: Annabelle borrows unabashedly from better horror films, content to leave viewers with a string of cheap jolts that fail to build on the far more effective The Conjuring.

title: Sacramento
rt_critics_consensus: An amusingly perceptive comedy about the push and pull of friendship, Sacramento glows with charm even when its characters drive each other mad.

title: Threads
rt_critics_consensus: An urgent warning against nuclear conflict, Threads is a chilling hypothetical that achieves visceral horror with its matter-of-fact presentation of an apocalypse.

title: The Watcher
rt_critics_consensus: The Watcher has Keanu Reeves cast against type, but the movie is short on thrills, suspense, and believability.

title: Three... Extremes
rt_critics_consensus: This anthology contains brutal, powerful horror stories by three of Asia's top directors.

title: The Last Days on Mars
rt_critics_consensus: Neither intelligent enough to work as thought-provoking sci-fi nor trashy enough to provide B-movie thrills, The Last Days on Mars proves as cinematically barren as the titular planet.

title: Fish Tank
rt_critics_consensus: Cannes Jury Prize-winner Fish Tank is gritty British realism at its very best, with flawless performances from newcomer Kate Jarvis, and Michael Fassbender.

title: The Righteous
rt_critics_consensus: Assured work from writer-director Mark O'Brien and Henry Czerny's powerhouse performance put The Righteous a cut above the average supernatural thriller.

title: Dracula's Daughter
rt_critics_consensus: Dracula's Daughter extends the Universal horror myth in an interesting direction, but the talky script and mild atmosphere undermine its ambition.

title: Hell of a Summer
rt_critics_consensus: Hell of a Summer shows some promising ingenuity from its young directorial duo, but its lack of tonal cohesion also indicates some room for improvement.

title: The Haunting of Sharon Tate
rt_critics_consensus: The Haunting of Sharon Tate dishonors the events it seeks to dramatize with a poorly acted and offensively exploitative take on real-life tragedy.

title: Creepy Tales of Pizza and Gore
Pride and Prejudice and Zombies
rt_critics_consensus: Pride and Prejudice and Zombies manages to wring a few fun moments out of its premise, but never delivers the thoroughly kooky mashup its title suggests.

title: The Little Girl Who Was Too Fond of Matches
Single White Female
rt_critics_consensus: Single White Female benefits from a pair of outstanding leads, neither of whom are well served by a storyline that wavers between thrillingly tense and utterly ridiculous.

title: Herr Arnes pengar (Sir Arne's Treasure)(Snows of Destiny)(The Three Who Were Doomed)
Antebellum
rt_critics_consensus: Antebellum fails to connect its images with any meaning, making for a largely unpleasant experience lacking any substantial scares.

title: The Last Time I Saw Richard
The Haunted Mansion
rt_critics_consensus: Neither scary nor funny, The Haunted Mansion is as lifeless as the ghosts in the movie.

title: Slow Burn
rt_critics_consensus: With wooden acting and hammy, overheated dialogue, Slow Burn isn't so much a noir as it is a mediocre parody of one.

title: Mr. Harrigan's Phone
rt_critics_consensus: Despite a pair of layered performances from its talented leads, Mr. Harrigan's Phone never quite connects with the source material's intriguing themes.

title: Werewolf by Night
rt_critics_consensus: A spooky yarn told with taut economy, Werewolf by Night is a standout Marvel entry that proves Michael Giacchino as atmospheric and skilled a director as he is a composer.

title: Blink Twice
rt_critics_consensus: A bold and memorable debut that thrusts Zoë Kravitz into the turf of directors to watch, Blink Twice is a live wire of a film.

title: Veronica
rt_critics_consensus: A scarily effective horror outing, Veronica proves it doesn't take fancy or exotic ingredients to craft skin-crawling genre thrills.

title: Alone in the Dead of Night
Menéndez: The Day of the Lord
The Return
rt_critics_consensus: A suspenseful but perplexing thriller.

title: Beyond Evil: The Dead of Night
Attachment
rt_critics_consensus: A possession thriller that knows the devil's in the details, Attachment scares some fresh angles out of a well-worn horror subgenre.

title: Nightmare at the End of the Hall
The Surrender
rt_critics_consensus: Extracting a maximum amount of creepy mood from a simple setup while reaching surprising poignancy as a mother-daughter story, The Surrender is a victorious feature debut from writer-director Julia Max.

title: Deep Rising
rt_critics_consensus: Its fleeting glimpses of creature feature mayhem are certainly dumb fun, but Deep Rising cribs from far too many better movies to stand on its own.

title: Bounce
rt_critics_consensus: Critics say Bounce is more of a thud. Plot turns feel cliched and contrived, and the romance between Paltrow and Affleck fails to engage.

title: Dead Meat Walking: A Zombie Walk Documentary
Send Help
rt_critics_consensus: Putting director Sam Raimi's penchant for diabolical mayhem to great use, Send Help doesn't need any assistance in thrills thanks to a very game Rachel McAdams and Dylan O'Brien along with a viciously clever script.

title: The Other Side of the Mirror
Planet Terror
rt_critics_consensus: A cool and hip grindhouse throwback, Planet Terror is an unpredictable zombie thrillride.

title: Akuma ga Kitarite Fue wo Fuku
The Muse
rt_critics_consensus: Despite quirky and original writing, the subject matter feels too removed to produce laughs.

title: Descent Into Darkness: My European Nightmare
I Am Lisa
rt_critics_consensus: A werewolf revenge flick with bite, I Am Lisa is a vibrant and intellectually nimble journey into female empowerment.

title: XX
rt_critics_consensus: XX's array of distinct female filmmaking voices -- and the empowerment their collection represents -- offer enough thrills to make up for the overall uneven quality that plagues most anthology projects.

title: A Light That Never Goes Out
Curse of Chucky
rt_critics_consensus: The franchise hex of disappointing sequels is broken by going back to basics in this chilling entry, restoring a sense of playfulness to the Chucky saga.

title: Superhost
rt_critics_consensus: Gracie Gillam's four-star performance helps Superhost roll out a blood red carpet for horror fans seeking delightfully grim accommodations.

title: The Haunting of the Murder House
Body Snatchers
rt_critics_consensus: Body Snatchers may not topple previous adaptions, but it boasts an effective sense of dread and strong characterizations.

title: Let Me In
rt_critics_consensus: Similar to the original in all the right ways -- but with enough changes to stand on its own -- Let Me In is the rare Hollywood remake that doesn't add insult to inspiration.

title: Scare Me
rt_critics_consensus: Smart, well-acted, and suitably chilling, Scare Me uses its familiar horror setting as the backdrop for a fresh deconstruction of standard genre ingredients.

title: Gremlins 2: The New Batch
rt_critics_consensus: Gremlins 2 trades the spiky thrills of its predecessor for looney satire, yielding a succession of sporadically clever gags that add some flavor to a recycled plot.

title: Mark of the Devil 777: The Moralist, Part 2
Nosferatu
rt_critics_consensus: One of the silent era's most influential masterpieces, Nosferatu's eerie, gothic feel -- and a chilling performance from Max Schreck as the vampire -- set the template for the horror films that followed.

title: I, Frankenstein
rt_critics_consensus: Loud, incoherent, and dramatically listless, I, Frankenstein is a remarkably dull fantasy adventure that fails to generate much excitement or interest in its characters.

title: Saw
rt_critics_consensus: Saw ensnares audiences with a deceptively clever plot and a myriad of memorable, nasty set pieces, but its lofty ambitions are undercut by a nihilistic streak that feels more mean than profound.

title: Kicking Blood
rt_critics_consensus: Kicking Blood overcomes evident budget limitations with solid acting and a vampire story that has an emotionally impactful twist.

title: Stalked by My Doctor: The Return
Devil
rt_critics_consensus: It's better than many of the other films M. Night Shyamalan has been associated with, but Devil never gets more than a few low-budget thrills out of its fiendishly promising premise.

title: Death Becomes Her
rt_critics_consensus: Hawn and Streep are as fabulous as Death Becomes Her's innovative special effects; Zemeckis' satire, on the other hand, is as hollow as the world it mocks.

title: Ghost Ship
rt_critics_consensus: With a plot as creaky as the boat, Ghost Ship fails to deliver the scares.

title: Dead Silence
rt_critics_consensus: More tasteful than recent slasher flicks, but Dead Silence is undone by boring characters, bland dialogue, and an unnecessary and obvious twist ending.

title: Halloween Kills
rt_critics_consensus: Halloween Kills should satisfy fans in search of brute slasher thrills, but in terms of advancing the franchise, it's a bit less than the sum of its bloody parts.

title: Prey for the Devil
rt_critics_consensus: There's no dearth of possession stories within the genre, and potboiler Prey for the Devil may leave completists needing a truly horrific -- or at least fun -- exorcist palate cleanser.

title: Army of Darkness
rt_critics_consensus: Some of the evil magic is gone as this trilogy capper dispenses with most of the scares, but Bruce Campbell's hammy charm and Sam Raimi's homage to classic visual effects make for a fun enough adventure.

title: Nadja
rt_critics_consensus: Nadja approaches the Dracula legend from an idiosyncratic angle - and with just enough visual style to overcome uneven storytelling.

title: Apollo 18
rt_critics_consensus: A boring, suspense-free Paranormal Activity rip-off that feels long even at just 90 minutes.

title: Blade II
rt_critics_consensus: Though Blade II offers more of what worked in the original, its plot and character development appear to have been left on the cutting room floor.

title: Apartment Zero
rt_critics_consensus: Despite some underserved and overwrought thematic elements, Apartment Zero emerges as a compellingly eerie thriller with strong lead performances and a stylish, neo-noir mood.

title: Shadow in the Cloud
rt_critics_consensus: Part creature feature, part war movie, and part social commentary, Shadow in the Cloud doesn't always blend its ingredients evenly -- but it's frequently pulpy fun.

title: Todd and the Book of Pure Evil: The End of the End
Da Sweet Blood of Jesus
rt_critics_consensus: Da Sweet Blood of Jesus has no shortage of style, but it isn't enough to make this horror-tinged Spike Lee joint one of his best -- or worth recommending.

title: Benny Loves You
rt_critics_consensus: A zany entry into the sentient murder toy genre, Benny Loves You is an enjoyably macabre and clever horror-comedy that will have audiences rooting for its loveable villain.

title: Slice and Dice: The Slasher Film Forever
Koko-di Koko-da
rt_critics_consensus: Using an effectively creepy setting and a beguilingly evasive approach, Koko-Di Koko-Da takes a chilling look at the long tail of grief.

title: Blue Hour: The Disappearance of Nick Brandreth
The Pope's Exorcist
rt_critics_consensus: Dominus Crowe! The Pope's Exorcist is standard-issue holy horror in most respects, but its star's sanctified performance will be the answer to some viewers' prayers.

title: Lady Belladonna's Tales From the Inferno
I Am Not a Serial Killer
rt_critics_consensus: I Am Not A Serial Killer honors the book it's based on with a well-acted drama that leavens its gore and dark themes with wry humor.

title: The Autopsy of Jane Doe
rt_critics_consensus: The Autopsy of Jane Doe subverts the gruesome expectations triggered by its title to deliver a smart, suggestively creepy thriller that bolsters director André Ovredal's growing reputation.

title: The Strangers: Chapter 2
rt_critics_consensus: A tired and tensionless slog, Chapter 2 squanders its potential for suspense with lazy storytelling, predictable scares, and a complete misunderstanding of what makes The Strangers so terrifying.

title: When a Stranger Calls
rt_critics_consensus: When a Stranger Calls ranks among the more misguided remakes in horror history, offering little more than a rote, largely fright-free update to the original.

title: The Evil Dead
rt_critics_consensus: So scrappy that it feels as illicit as a book found in the woods, The Evil Dead is a stomach-churning achievement in bad taste that marks a startling debut for wunderkind Sam Raimi.

title: Black Cat In a Dark Room

rt_critics_consensus: Relying on psychological tension rather than overt violence and gore, 1408 is a genuinely creepy thriller with a strong lead performance by John Cusack.

title: Gothika
rt_critics_consensus: Berry's acting talents can't save Gothika from its preposterous plot and bad dialogue.

title: The First Omen
rt_critics_consensus: Frequently frightening even as it plays within the confines of a nearly 50-year-old film series, this prequel is The First Omen of a bright future for the franchise in quite some time.

title: Hair High
rt_critics_consensus: Hair High isn't first-tier Plympton, but like the rest of the animator's work, this is an assuredly odd tale that should resonate with fans of strange cinema.

title: The Strange Vice of Mrs. Wardh
Anita - ur en tonårsflickas dagbok (Anita: Swedish Nymphet) (Anita)
Down to the Bone
rt_critics_consensus: A vivid portrayal of the effects of drug abuse that avoids cinematic clichés, Down to the Bone is a winning effort that features Vera Farmiga's breakout performance.

title: I'm No Longer Here
rt_critics_consensus: I'm No Longer Here's occasionally uneven narrative is more than offset by its honest and visually poetic approach to themes of identity and assimilation.

title: Papaya: Love Goddess of the Cannibals
Beyond Horror: The History and Sub-Culture of Red Films
Would You Rather
rt_critics_consensus: Though restrained by most torture porn standards, Would You Rather's brilliant premise is ultimately harmed by its tendency towards blood and guts.

title: Jaws the Revenge
rt_critics_consensus: Illogical, tension-free, and filled with cut-rate special effects, Jaws: The Revenge is a sorry chapter in a once-proud franchise.

title: Saint Maud
rt_critics_consensus: A brilliantly unsettling blend of body horror and psychological thriller, Saint Maud marks an impressive debut for writer-director Rose Glass.

title: Demon of Steel: Battle of the Great Demon Beasts
Eraserhead
rt_critics_consensus: David Lynch's surreal Eraserhead uses detailed visuals and a creepy score to create a bizarre and disturbing look into a man's fear of parenthood.

title: Dreamcatcher
rt_critics_consensus: An incoherent and overly long creature feature.

title: Plan 9 From Outer Space
rt_critics_consensus: The epitome of so-bad-it's-good cinema, Plan 9 From Outer Space is an unintentionally hilarious sci-fi "thriller" from anti-genius Ed Wood that is justly celebrated for its staggering ineptitude.

title: Blood Hunters: Rise of the Hybrids
Dracula Untold
rt_critics_consensus: Neither awful enough to suck nor sharp enough to bite, Dracula Untold misses the point of its iconic character's deathless appeal.

title: Do You Wanna Know a Secret?
V/H/S/Beyond
rt_critics_consensus: The V/H/S series shows no signs of the tape wearing out with this terrifying entry, boasting an assortment of shorts that'll sate the appetite of horror and sci-fi aficionados alike.

title: Rakht: What If You Can See the Future
The Mean One
rt_critics_consensus: A killjoy entry into the debauched children's story horror canon, The Mean One delivers a seasonally macabre bag of lime green coal.

title: I Saw the Face of the Devil
Pet Sematary Two
rt_critics_consensus: Not realizing that it had no unfinished business, Pet Sematary rises from the grave once more to beat an undead corpse.

title: The Forsaken
rt_critics_consensus: It's all been done before, and done better.

title: Let's Not Meet in the Woods
Taxi
rt_critics_consensus: Silly and unfunny remake of a French movie of the same name.

title: Red State
rt_critics_consensus: Red State is an audacious and brash affair that ultimately fails to provide competent scares or thrills.

title: Urban Legends: Final Cut
rt_critics_consensus: This teen horror movie brings nothing new to an already exhausted genre. And it's bad. Really bad.

title: The Naughty List of Mr. Scrooge
The Beast With a Million Eyes
The Night Listener
rt_critics_consensus: This psychological thriller compels by blurring the line between truth and fiction; unfortunately, the film itself gets muddled in a hazy account of Maupin's original novel.

title: Dog Soldiers
rt_critics_consensus: Frightening, funny, and packed with action, Dog Soldiers is well worth checking out for genre fans -- and marks writer-director Neil Marshall as a talent to keep an eye on.

title: Alien: Covenant
rt_critics_consensus: Alien: Covenant delivers another satisfying round of close-quarters deep-space terror, even if it doesn't take the saga in any new directions.

title: Mercy Falls - How Far Would You Fall To Survive?
The Third Saturday in October: Part V
Knock at the Cabin
rt_critics_consensus: Although it's often less than scary and parts of the story don't bear scrutiny, Knock at the Cabin is a thought-provoking chiller and upper-tier Shyamalan.

title: Squeak and I'll Run to You
Trash Humpers
rt_critics_consensus: It's oddly affecting in a deeply discomfiting way, but Trash Humpers pales in comparison with Harmony Korine's earlier, truly transgressive work.

title: The Legend of Robert the Doll
Scare Package II: Rad Chad's Revenge
The Last House on the Beach
Wolfen
rt_critics_consensus: Police procedural meets werewolf flick in Wolfen, a creepy creature feature with a surprisingly profound side.

title: I Walked With a Zombie
rt_critics_consensus: Evocative direction by Jacques Tourneur collides with the low-rent production values of exploitateer Val Lewton in I Walked with a Zombie, a sultry sleeper that's simultaneously smarmy, eloquent and fascinating.

title: Pet Sematary
rt_critics_consensus: Pet Sematary is a bruising horror flick that wears its quirks on its sleeves, to the detriment of its scare factor.

title: The Werewolf Game: The Beast Side
Carnival of Souls
rt_critics_consensus: Carnival of Souls offers delightfully chilling proof that when it comes to telling an effective horror story, less can often be much, much more.

title: Tales of Terror -The Curse of Nobuhiro-san-
Revenge in the House of Usher
Slapface
rt_critics_consensus: Slapface's uneven execution is offset by the thoughtful themes that fuel the monstrous developments in its character-driven horror story.

title: I Don't Understand You
rt_critics_consensus: Amusing as a satire of Americans abroad but less convincing as a horror hybrid, I Don't Understand You gets some mileage from Andrew Rannells and Nick Kroll's chemistry but can't quite overcome its tonal language barrier.

title: Tales From the Darkside: The Movie
My Heart Can't Beat Unless You Tell It To
rt_critics_consensus: Unsettling and compelling in equal measure, My Heart Can't Beat Unless You Tell It To casts a visually striking and thought-provoking spell.

title: The Grotesque World of Jimmy the Bean
Scouts Guide to the Zombie Apocalypse
rt_critics_consensus: Scouts Guide to the Zombie Apocalypse fails to live up to its intriguingly wacky title, instead delivering yet another zombie comedy-thriller with a tired T&A twist.

title: Satan Place: A Soap Opera From Hell
The Last Five Days: The Freak Building
Poultrygeist: Night of the Chicken Dead
rt_critics_consensus: Poultrygeist may be relentlessly tasteless and juvenile, but it's also a lively slice of schlocky fun.

title: Witchcraft X: Mistress of the Craft
Puppet Master: The Littlest Reich
rt_critics_consensus: Puppet Master: The Littlest Reich delivers all the proudly distasteful B-movie antics genre fans might expect from a film about murderous Nazi dolls.

title: Color Out of Space
rt_critics_consensus: A welcome return for director Richard Stanley, Color Out of Space mixes tart B-movie pulp with visually alluring Lovecraftian horror and a dash of gonzo Nicolas Cage.

title: Bad Hair
rt_critics_consensus: Immersive and powerfully acted, Bad Hair offers an unflinching yet gently endearing depiction of poverty's impact on youthful self-actualization.

title: Death Bed: The Bed That Eats
The Last Matinee
rt_critics_consensus: The Last Matinee is a blood-soaked homage to classic slasher cinema that delivers thrills, style, and spectacular gore.

title: Return to... Return to Nuke 'Em High AKA Volume 2
Piranha 3DD
rt_critics_consensus: It strains to up the gore and self-awareness of it predecessor, and -- despite some game celebrity cameos -- the result is a dispiriting echo of 2010's horror-comedy.

title: Choke
rt_critics_consensus: While bolstered by strong performances from Sam Rockwell and Anjelica Huston, Choke struggles to capture the tone of Chuck Palahniuk's novel.

title: Winners Tape All: The Henderson Brothers Story
The Boogie Man Will Get You
Buffy the Vampire Slayer
rt_critics_consensus: Buffy the Vampire Slayer's supernatural coming of age tale is let down by poor directing and even poorer plotting -- though Kristy Swanson and Paul Reubens' game performances still manage to slay.

title: Bride of Chucky
rt_critics_consensus: Bride of Chucky is devoid of any fright and the franchise has become tiresomely self-parodic, although horror fans may find some pleasure in this fourth entry's camp factor.

title: The Black Phone
rt_critics_consensus: The Black Phone might have been even more frightening, but it remains an entertaining, well-acted adaptation of scarily good source material.

title: You Are Not My Mother
rt_critics_consensus: Smartly creepy, You Are Not My Mother engages with a number of thought-provoking themes without sacrificing chills.

title: The Meg
rt_critics_consensus: The Meg sets audiences up for a good old-fashioned B-movie creature feature, but lacks the genre thrills -- or the cheesy bite -- to make it worth diving in.

title: Tamara
rt_critics_consensus: Resolutely misguided without ever really crossing into "so bad it's good" territory, Tamara lacks even the cheap thrills promised by its premise.

title: Emanuelle and the White Slave Trade
The ABCs of Death
rt_critics_consensus: As is often the case with anthology films, The ABCs of Death is wildly uneven, with several legitimately scary entries and a bunch more that miss the mark.

title: Roxanne
rt_critics_consensus: Though its sweetness borders on sappiness, Roxanne is an unabashedly romantic comedy that remains one of Steve Martin's funniest films.

title: The Bye Bye Man
rt_critics_consensus: The Bye Bye Man clumsily mashes together elements from better horror films, adding up to a derivative effort as short on originality as it is on narrative coherency or satisfying scares.

title: Disturbia
rt_critics_consensus: Aside from its clichéd resolution, Disturbia is a tense, subtle thriller with a noteworthy performance from Shia LaBeouf.

title: My Best Friend's Exorcism
rt_critics_consensus: My Best Friend's Exorcism has some fun moments, but ultimately collapses into a dull, unoriginal 80s-style possession story with weak effects and misplaced comedy.

title: Home with a View of the Monster
My Best Friend Is a Vampire
Midnighters
rt_critics_consensus: Adding dashes of horror to a noir premise, Midnighters is a genre-bending chiller that delights as often as it scares.

title: Dead Man Tells His Own Tale (El Muerto Cuenta su Historia)
Deathgasm
rt_critics_consensus: Deathgasm plumbs the blood-spattered depths of grindhouse gore with enough giddy glee to satisfy genre enthusiasts looking for a thrill.

title: Tess
rt_critics_consensus: A reverent adaptation of Thomas Hardy's novel, Tess marries painterly cinematography and unhurried pacing to create an epic ode to perseverance.

title: Night of the Living Dead: Resurrection
Otto; or Up With Dead People
rt_critics_consensus: Otto, or Up with Dead People plays to writer-director Bruce LaBruce's provocative strengths, but proves frustratingly less than the sum of its parts.

title: The Intervention
rt_critics_consensus: The Intervention's familiar plot is enlivened by complex character dynamics and a likable cast.

title: Paranormal Activity 2
rt_critics_consensus: Paranormal Activity 2 doesn't cover any new ground, but its premise is still scary -- and in some respects, it's a better film than the original.

title: Fur: An Imaginary Portrait of Diane Arbus
rt_critics_consensus: This portrait of a groundbreaking photographer lacks the daring of its subject.

title: The Mummy and the Curse of the Jackals
Curse of Crom: The Legend of Halloween
Passion Play
rt_critics_consensus: Passion Play has a terrific cast, but don't be fooled - the only real question at the heart of this misbegotten mystery is what its stars were thinking.

title: The First Wave
rt_critics_consensus: The First Wave presents a starkly gripping snapshot of a crisis unfolding -- and its grim toll on those who put their lives on the line to fight it.

title: Bhoot: Part One: The Haunted Ship
Camp Blood 666 Part 2: Exorcism of the Clown
Insidious: The Red Door
rt_critics_consensus: Earlier installments have had their moments, but behind Insidious: The Red Door lies the disappointing denouement of a once-frightening franchise.

title: The Outsiders
rt_critics_consensus: The cracks continue to show in Coppola's directorial style, but The Outsiders remains a blustery, weird, and fun adaptation of the classic novel.

title: Cronos
rt_critics_consensus: Guillermo del Toro's unique feature debut is not only gory and stylish, but also charming and intelligent.

title: Me and My Mates vs. The Zombie Apocalypse
The Last Kiss
rt_critics_consensus: Well-preformed, but it's nothing we haven't seen before.

title: The Devil's Bath
rt_critics_consensus: A squirm-inducing period piece that locates true horror in both mind and spirit, The Devil's Bath might be Severin Fiala and Veronika Franz's most chilling directorial effort yet.

title: The Walker
rt_critics_consensus: Despite a strong performance from Woody Harrelson, The Walker can be slow and dull at times, detracting from the talented cast.

title: Behind the Mask: The Rise of Leslie Vernon
rt_critics_consensus: A smart mockumentary that presents a gory, funny, and obviously affectionate skewering of the slasher genre.

title: One Cup on the Dead Track
Bloodsucka Jones vs. the Creeping Death
The Amusement Park
rt_critics_consensus: A blunt yet visceral depiction of society's treatment of the elderly, The Amusement Park sees George A. Romero exploring a different -- yet still chilling -- type of terror.

title: Couples Retreat
rt_critics_consensus: Despite a talented cast and some reliably pleasant interplay between Jon Favreau and Vince Vaughn, Couples Retreat leaves viewers stranded in an arid, mirthless comedy.

title: Sharknado 3: Oh Hell No!
rt_critics_consensus: Sharknado 3: Oh Hell No! bites off more than it can chew, leaving viewers with an overlong mess that isn't even bad enough to be good.

title: Trolls
rt_critics_consensus: Trolls brings its instantly recognizable characters to the big screen in a colorful adventure that, while geared toward the younger set, isn't without rewards for parents.

title: Piranha
rt_critics_consensus: Playing exactly to expectations for a movie about killer fish run amok, Piranha 3-D dishes out gore, guffaws and gratuitous nudity with equal glee.

title: Mary Reilly
rt_critics_consensus: The movie looks good and has its moments. But, overall, the movie borders on boredom.

title: The Shrouds
rt_critics_consensus: Ruminating on the love within loss, The Shrouds is a personal and peculiar examination of grief by director David Cronenberg.

title: Mad God
rt_critics_consensus: A rich visual treat for film fans, Mad God proves that even in the age of CGI, the cinematic allure of stop-motion animation remains strong.

title: Bad Behaviour
rt_critics_consensus: Despite impressive work from Jennifer Connelly and Alice Englert, Bad Behaviour is too scattered and disjointed to support its unwieldy ambitions.

title: Damien: Omen II
rt_critics_consensus: Damien dishes out ghoulish scares and a biblical body count to generate some morbid fun, but this repetitious sequel lacks the sophistication of its predecessor.

title: Blade: Trinity
rt_critics_consensus: Louder, campier, and more incoherent than its predecessors, Blade: Trinity seems content to emphasize style over substance and rehash familiar themes.

title: Nightmare Alley
rt_critics_consensus: Playing against type with Nightmare Alley, Tyrone Power and Edmund Goulding deliver some of their best work in a carnival-set noir unafraid to showcase true despair.

title: Devon's Ghost: Legend of the Bloody Boy
100 Ghost Street: The Return of Richard Speck
Videodrome
rt_critics_consensus: Visually audacious, disorienting, and just plain weird, Videodrome's musings on technology, entertainment, and politics still feel fresh today.

title: Bella
rt_critics_consensus: Critics labeled Bella as a simplistic and mostly pedestrian, but positive word of mouth gave this tiny indie surprising theatrical legs.

title: Wind Chill
rt_critics_consensus: Wind Chill is a ghost story with a clunky and unpolished script that fails to keep viewers in suspense.

title: Rounding
rt_critics_consensus: Even though Rounding doesn't fully live up to its best expectations, this psychological drama creeps under the skin thanks in part to Namir Smallwood's winning performance.

title: Elizabeth: The Golden Age
rt_critics_consensus: This sequel is full of lavish costumes and elaborate sets, but lacks the heart and creativity of the original Elizabeth

title: Hoot
rt_critics_consensus: Lacking energy and humor, Hoot is a ho-hum story of eco-awareness that falls flat as a pancake.

title: Stake Land
rt_critics_consensus: Though the genre is well worn at this point, director Jim Mickle focuses on strong characterization and eerie atmosphere to craft an effective apocalyptic vampire chiller that also manages to pack a mean punch.

title: On the Road
rt_critics_consensus: Beautiful to look at but a bit too respectfully crafted, On the Road doesn't capture the energy and inspiration of Jack Kerouac's novel.

title: Deadly Friend
rt_critics_consensus: An uninspired departure for Wes Craven, mired by an uneven premise; beware, this is one Deadly Friend.

title: Cat's Eye
rt_critics_consensus: An effective if knowingly silly Stephen King anthology that combines comedy and terror.

title: The Devils
rt_critics_consensus: Grimly stylish, Ken Russell's baroque opus is both provocative and persuasive in its contention that the greatest blasphemy is the leveraging of faith for power.

title: The Darkness, the Rage and the Fury
Hereafter
rt_critics_consensus: Despite a thought-provoking premise and Clint Eastwood's typical flair as director, Hereafter fails to generate much compelling drama, straddling the line between poignant sentimentality and hokey tedium.

title: Insidious: Chapter 2
rt_critics_consensus: Insidious: Chapter 2 is decidedly short on the tension and surprises that made its predecessor so chilling.

title: Marrowbone
rt_critics_consensus: Marrowbone's effective setting and strong cast can't make up for thinly conceived characters and a story short on genuine scares.

title: Open Water
rt_critics_consensus: A low budget thriller with some intense moments.

title: Saw II
rt_critics_consensus: Saw II is likely to please the gore-happy fans of the original, though it may be too gruesome for those not familiar with first film's premise.

title: Bloody Muscle Body Builder in Hell
From Dusk Till Dawn 3: The Hangman's Daughter
Godsend
rt_critics_consensus: A murky thriller with few chills, Godsend features ludicrous dialogue, by-the-numbers plotting, and an excess of cheap shocks.

title: Tumbbad
rt_critics_consensus: Tumbbad has everything you never knew you needed in a cliché horror romp that is very pleasing to the eye.

title: Hush Little Baby: Welcome to the Family
No Man's Land: The Rise of Reeker
Koji Shiraishi's Never Send Me, Please
Prevenge
rt_critics_consensus: As ambitious as it is daringly transgressive, Prevenge should thrill fans of pitch-black horror-comedy -- and open untold opportunities for writer/director/star Alice Lowe.

title: We Bury the Dead
rt_critics_consensus: Anchored by Daisy Ridley's magnetic performance and a grimly inventive premise, We Bury the Dead keenly uses familiar zombie tropes as a framework to deliver a beautifully shot, emotionally resonant meditation on loss and grief.

title: The Belko Experiment
rt_critics_consensus: The Belko Experiment offers a few moments of lurid fun for genre enthusiasts, but lacks enough subversive smarts to consistently engage once the carnage kicks in.

title: Self-Help
rt_critics_consensus: Self-Help cements director Erik Bloomquist's knack for offbeat horror, weaving together gore, humor, and familial unease into a deliriously twisted genre mashup.

title: Ouija: Origin of Evil
rt_critics_consensus: Ouija: Origin of Evil swerves its franchise's planchette unexpectedly to YES with a surprisingly scary and dramatically satisfying follow-up to its lackluster predecessor.

title: May
rt_critics_consensus: Above average slasher flick.

title: Hellraiser
rt_critics_consensus: Elevated by writer-director Clive Barker's fiendishly unique vision, Hellraiser offers a disquieting - and sadistically smart - alternative to mindless gore.

title: Martyrs Lane
rt_critics_consensus: Well-acted by its young leads, Martyrs Lane tells a slow-burning ghost story that gathers real emotional weight.

title: Weapons
rt_critics_consensus: Zach Cregger spins an expertly crafted yarn of terrifying mystery and thrilling intrigue in Weapons, a sophomore triumph that solidifies his status as a master of horror.

title: Pater Noster and the Mission of Light
Murder Ballads: How to Make It in Rock 'n' Roll
Spoor
rt_critics_consensus: Spoor stylishly taps into a deep well of anger to deliver an unusual yet largely effective revenge thriller with environmental and feminist overtones.

title: Tetsudon: The ABCs of Fool Japan
Wrong Turn
rt_critics_consensus: An unremarkable slasher flick that fails to distinguished itself from others of its ilk.

title: It Came From Outer Space
rt_critics_consensus: It Came From Outer Space is a pulpy-but-effective psychological sci-fi picture, with a provocative message about xenophobia.

title: Terrifier 2
rt_critics_consensus: Terrifier 2 outdoes the original in every way -- which makes it bad news for the squeamish, but a bloody good time for genre enthusiasts.

title: Hero
rt_critics_consensus: With death-defying action sequences and epic historic sweep, Hero offers everything a martial arts fan could ask for.

title: From Dusk Till Dawn
rt_critics_consensus: A pulpy crime drama/vampire film hybrid, From Dusk Till Dawn is an uneven but often deliriously enjoyable B-movie.

title: The Possession of Hannah Grace
rt_critics_consensus: The Possession of Hannah Grace feints at real horror just often enough to offer hints of the movie it should have been -- and further frustrate viewers seeking a good scare.

title: Kingdom Come
rt_critics_consensus: While it has its moments, Kingdom Come is marred by a script that's low on laughs and uneven in tone.

title: The Greasy Strangler
rt_critics_consensus: The Greasy Strangler definitely isn't for everyone, but filmgoers in step with its off-kilter stride are in store for a singular cinematic experience.

title: Rosemary's Baby
rt_critics_consensus: A frightening tale of Satanism and pregnancy that is even more disturbing than it sounds thanks to convincing and committed performances by Mia Farrow and Ruth Gordon.

title: Train to Busan
rt_critics_consensus: Train to Busan delivers a thrillingly unique -- and purely entertaining -- take on the zombie genre, with fully realized characters and plenty of social commentary to underscore the bursts of skillfully staged action.

title: Backcountry
rt_critics_consensus: Tense, well-acted, and at once atmospheric as well as brutally impactful, Backcountry marks a memorably assured debut from writer-director Adam MacDonald.

title: Urban Legend
rt_critics_consensus: Elements of Scream reappear in a vastly inferior vehicle.

title: Jennifer's Body
rt_critics_consensus: Jennifer's Body features occasionally clever dialogue, but its horror/comedy premise ultimately fails to be consistently funny or scary enough to satisfy.

title: Bite Me If You Love Me
I Will Never Leave You Alone
Nina Forever
rt_critics_consensus: Nina Forever tests the limits of the rom-com with a decidedly unorthodox triangle that's as diabolically original as it is daringly dark.

title: Eye See You
rt_critics_consensus: Whether it's being presented as D-Tox or Eye See You, this Stallone starring vehicle is a slapdash thriller to actively avoid.

title: The Haunting in Connecticut 2: Ghosts of Georgia
The House With a Clock in Its Walls
rt_critics_consensus: An entertaining PG detour for gore maestro Eli Roth, The House with a Clock in Its Walls is a family-friendly blend of humor and horror with an infectious sense of fun.

title: The King
rt_critics_consensus: The King pursues a wildly ambitious thesis through some fairly bumpy territory, but emerges as a provocative and insightful look at modern America.

title: Don't Look Back... or You'll be Sorry!
MadS
rt_critics_consensus: Immersing audiences in pure pandemonium with technical ingenuity and an unblinking point-of-view, MadS is a thrill ride that never lets up.

title: Bad Things
rt_critics_consensus: There are good things and there are Bad Things, and while the film may not rise above its potential -- or source of inspiration -- it delivers a memorable experience.

title: Mother of Flies
rt_critics_consensus: Somber and boldly intimate, Mother of Flies is a folk horror chamber piece that infuses its meditation on death with jaw-dropping practical effects and ferocious weirdness.

title: Cow
rt_critics_consensus: A stark and emotionally resonant look at the life of a farm animal, Cow simply yet insistently asks the viewer to reconsider their relationship with their food.

title: Senritsu Kaiki File Super Kowa Too! Fear Adventure: Kokkuri-San
World War Z
rt_critics_consensus: It's uneven and diverges from the source book, but World War Z still brings smart, fast-moving thrills and a solid performance from Brad Pitt to the zombie genre.

title: Unknown Dimension: The Story of Paranormal Activity
Carnage Collection: Puppet Master: Trunk Full of Terror
All Jacked Up and Full of Worms
rt_critics_consensus: For better or worse depending on your taste, All Jacked Up and Full of Worms is just as grimy and transgressive as one might expect given its title.

title: Ichi the Killer
rt_critics_consensus: Ichi The Killer is a thoroughly shocking gorefest that will surely entertain those with strong stomachs and a penchant for brutal violence.

title: 2,000 Maniacs
rt_critics_consensus: It didn't take much to thrill early splatter fans, and Two Thousand Maniacs! proves it with its shrill soundtrack, basement-level theatrics, and goofy flesh-tearing gore.

title: Rattlesnake
rt_critics_consensus: Carmen Ejogo is stranded in a wasteland both figuratively and literally in this disposable thriller, which lacks the cleverness or specificity to give its thin conceit any bite.

title: Diggers
rt_critics_consensus: Though the plot may be familiar, Diggers is just pleasant, charming, and heartwarming enough to make it worth your while.

title: DOTGA: Da One That Ghost Away
Body Cam
rt_critics_consensus: Ambitious yet undercooked, Body Cam can't quite connect the dots between its genre thrills and socially aware themes.

title: PG: Psycho Goreman
rt_critics_consensus: Over the top and enthusiastically strange, PG: Psycho Goreman delivers all the cheesy midnight-movie goodness promised by its title.

title: The Navy vs. the Night Monsters
Don't Look Now
rt_critics_consensus: Don't Look Now patiently builds suspense with haunting imagery and a chilling score -- causing viewers to feel Donald Sutherland and Julie Christie's grief deep within.

title: Isis Rising: Curse of the Lady Mummy
Rise of the Scarecrows: Hell on Earth
Cycle of Fear: There Is No End
Beach Massacre at Kill Devil Hills
Black Sheep
rt_critics_consensus: With an outrageous premise played completely straight, Black Sheep is a violent, grotesque, and very funny movie that takes B-movie lunacy to a delirious extreme.

title: Let the Right One In
rt_critics_consensus: Let the Right One In reinvigorates the seemingly tired vampire genre by effectively mixing scares with intelligent storytelling.

title: The Similars
rt_critics_consensus: A smart homage to genre filmmaking, The Similars is a fun and frightening film that balances socio-political issues with aplomb.

title: Starry Eyes
rt_critics_consensus: Starry Eyes pokes Hollywood's seedy underbelly to produce a refreshingly original horror story led by a breakout performance from Alex Essoe.

title: Candy
rt_critics_consensus: Stars Heath Ledger and Abbie Cornish look better than they should as heroin addicts, and their characters are too absorbed and self-pitying to be totally compelling.

title: The Night of the Hunter
rt_critics_consensus: Featuring Robert Mitchum's formidable performance as a child-hunting preacher, The Night of the Hunter is a disturbing look at good and evil.

title: I Trapped the Devil
rt_critics_consensus: A slow-burning horror story that takes spine-tingling advantage of its setting and cast, I Trapped the Devil marks an auspicious debut for writer-director Josh Lobo.

title: Shadow of the Vampire
rt_critics_consensus: Shadow of the Vampire is frightening, compelling, and funny, and features an excellent performance by Willem Dafoe.

title: Zombie Strippers
rt_critics_consensus: Though intentionally campy, Zombie Strippers suffers from poor execution, and never rises above its silly premise.

title: When Animals Dream
rt_critics_consensus: This Danish feast may leave you wanting more, but When Animals Dream remains a solid attempt to redefine female adolescence through a lycanthropic lens.

title: The Inconsiderables: Last Exit Out of Hollywood
The Pale Blue Eye
rt_critics_consensus: The Pale Blue Eye lacks its source material's piercing gaze, but this well-cast mystery is just intriguing enough to investigate.

title: The Wolf Man
rt_critics_consensus: A handsomely told tale with an affecting performance from Lon Chaney, Jr., The Wolf Man remains one of the classics of the Universal horror stable.

title: Hereditary
rt_critics_consensus: Hereditary uses its classic setup as the framework for a harrowing, uncommonly unsettling horror film whose cold touch lingers long beyond the closing credits.

title: The Lost Vlog of Ruby Real
Meg 2: The Trench
rt_critics_consensus: It isn't without its fun moments, but Meg 2: The Trench suffers from a disjointed story that drifts for too long before finally delivering a few campy thrills.

title: Come to Daddy
rt_critics_consensus: Bloody horror with barbed wit, Come to Daddy anchors its brutal violence in a surprisingly mature approach to provocative themes.

title: Midsommar
rt_critics_consensus: Ambitious, impressively crafted, and above all unsettling, Midsommar further proves writer-director Ari Aster is a horror auteur to be reckoned with.

title: Final Destination
rt_critics_consensus: Despite a panel of X-Files' alums at the helm and a promising premise, flighty performances and poor execution keep Final Destination from ever taking off.

title: Southbound
rt_critics_consensus: Southbound doesn't entirely avoid the jarring shifts common to anthology films, but thanks to some thrilling twists and turns, this horror road movie is a surprisingly smooth ride.

title: Twilight Zone: The Movie
rt_critics_consensus: Twilight Zone: The Movie suffers from the typical anthology-film highs and lows -- thankfully, the former outnumber the latter.

title: Velvet Buzzsaw
rt_critics_consensus: If you only watch one art-world satire with horror overtones this year -- or most others -- it should probably be Velvet Buzzsaw.

title: Horse Girl
rt_critics_consensus: Horse Girl proves unwilling or unable to explore the deeper themes it addresses, but this unusual drama is anchored by Alison Brie's committed performance.

title: Monster Summer
rt_critics_consensus: Monster Summer offers some decent PG scares and an appealing cast, yet ultimately feels like a diluted, slightly clunky imitation of its genre influences.

title: The Amazing Adventures of the Living Corpse
Yakuza Apocalypse
