rt_critics_consensus: Yakuza Apocalypse lives up to the oddball promise of its title with a cinematic whirlwind that represents director Takashi Miike at his most delightfully unhinged.

title: The Crow
rt_critics_consensus: Filled with style and dark, lurid energy, The Crow is an action-packed visual feast that also has a soul in the performance of the late Brandon Lee.

title: Paranormal Activity: Next of Kin
rt_critics_consensus: Paranormal Activity: Next of Kin gives the long-running found-footage franchise a new level of visual polish; unfortunately, effective scares are few and far between.

title: DC Showcase: Constantine: The House of Mystery
The Other Side of the Door
rt_critics_consensus: Laden with flimsy jump scares and cheap stereotypes, The Other Side of the Door wastes solid work from Sarah Wayne Callies on thoroughly middling horror fare.

title: Let Us Go and Burn Her Body; Or, The Devil Done Let Out
Silent Hill
rt_critics_consensus: Silent Hill is visually impressive, but as with many video game adaptations, it's plagued by inane dialogue, a muddled plot, and an overlong runtime.

title: Jethica
rt_critics_consensus: Packing a lot into its brief runtime, Jethica anchors its supernatural thrills with the serious story of a truly terrifying stalker.

title: 13 Sins
rt_critics_consensus: 13 Sins may be derivative of other horror films that made their moral points with more finesse, but it atones with a grim sense of humor and sleek style.

title: The Hole in the Ground
rt_critics_consensus: The Hole in the Ground artfully exploits parental fears with a well-made horror outing that makes up in sheer effectiveness what it lacks in originality.

title: 6 Souls
rt_critics_consensus: The most mind-bending aspect of 6 Souls is Julianne Moore's participation, the overqualified star wasted on a goofy horror premise that generates more guffaws than scares.

title: Heart Eyes
rt_critics_consensus: A mixture of gory slasher and sweet rom-com that ingeniously nails both formulas, Heart Eyes serves up a bloody valentine that'll make the heart skip a beat.

title: Spread
rt_critics_consensus: Despite occasional detours into surprisingly dark territory, Spread overall is an ineffectual celebration of vacuous Los Angeles high life rather than a deconstruction of it.

title: The Beach Girls and the Monster
Paranormal Activity: The Ghost Dimension
rt_critics_consensus: Paranormal Activity: The Ghost Dimension ties up some of the franchise's lingering questions, but six films into the series, the thrills are mostly gone.

title: Paranormal Farm 2 Closer To The Truth
Exorcist: The Beginning
rt_critics_consensus: A mediocre, gory horror film, nowhere near the quality of the 1973 original.

title: Bone Lake
rt_critics_consensus: Unwavering in its psychological trappings, Bone Lake is a supercharged game of survival that yields a thrilling rabbit-hole of intrigue and fun.

title: Relic
rt_critics_consensus: Relic ratchets up its slowly building tension in an expertly crafted atmosphere of dread, adding up to an outstanding feature debut for director/co-writer Natalie Erika James.

title: Sick of Myself
rt_critics_consensus: Sick of Myself is intent on turning stomachs with its vicious satire of vanity in the time of social media, delivering a dark one-upping saga of egos gone awry.

title: Dead Ringers
rt_critics_consensus: Dead Ringers serves up a double dose of Jeremy Irons in service of a devilishly unsettling concept and commandingly creepy work from director David Cronenberg.

title: Control Room
rt_critics_consensus: A fascinating, enlightening behind-the-scenes look at the Al Jazeera network.

title: Five Nights at Freddy's 2
rt_critics_consensus: A step down from an already clunky original, Five Nights at Freddy's 2 goes through the motions with all the grace of a malfunctioning animatronic.

title: Nightbreed
rt_critics_consensus: Nightbreed's imaginative world-building and startling creature designs are no match for its clumsy, uneven plotting.

title: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions
rt_critics_consensus: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions may appeal to fans of the original who've been hoping for a sequel, but its increasingly convoluted rules add up to a very unpleasant game night.

title: Senritsu Kaiki File Kowasugi File 04: The Truth! Hanako-san in the Toilet
Nosferatu the Vampyre
rt_critics_consensus: Stunning visuals from Werner Herzog and an intense portrayal of the famed bloodsucker from Klaus Kinski make Nosferatu the Vampyre a horror classic in its own right.

title: Pumpkinhead
rt_critics_consensus: With effects work and solid direction from Stan Winston -- and Lance Henriksen adding welcome gravitas -- Pumpkinhead is a creature feature that stands a cut above.

title: Vulgar
rt_critics_consensus: Vulgar more than lives up to its title.

title: Dead Mail
rt_critics_consensus: Punching above its limited budget's weight with an intriguing hook and inventive style, Dead Mail is a polished thriller that audiences won't be returning to sender.

title: The Great and Terrible Day of the Lord
Sicko
rt_critics_consensus: Driven by Michael Moore's sincere humanism, Sicko is a devastating, convincing, and very entertaining documentary about the state of America's health care.

title: Book of Shadows: Blair Witch 2
rt_critics_consensus: This sequel to Blair Witch Project is all formula and no creativity, mechanically borrowing elements from the original and other horror movies.

title: God's Left Hand, Devil's Right Hand
Yella
rt_critics_consensus: Chilly and haunting, Yella's atmosphere gets under the skin.

title: Pyewacket
rt_critics_consensus: Pyewacket builds its atmospheric, patiently encroaching chills on a well-told story concerned more with emotional trauma than supernatural threats.

title: Orpheus
rt_critics_consensus: Heavy with symbolism and deliberately paced, Orpheus may not be for everyone -- but as an example of Jean Cocteau's eccentric genius, it's all but impossible not to recommend.

title: Daisy Derkins vs. The Bloodthirsty Beast of Barren Pines!
Rec 3: Genesis
rt_critics_consensus: [REC] 3 Genesis discards the handheld POV of its predecessors and loses the franchise's fresh perspective in the bargain, upping the gags and gore to the detriment of the chills that audiences crave.

title: BloodRayne
rt_critics_consensus: BloodRayne is an absurd sword-and-sorcery vid-game adaptation from schlock-maestro Uwe Boll, featuring a distinguished (and slumming) cast.

title: Huesera: The Bone Woman
rt_critics_consensus: A bone-chilling body horror, Huesera offers genre fans a twisted take on What to Expect When You're Expecting.

title: Three Days in the Woods 2: Killin' Time
Gamera: The Guardian of the Universe
Friday the 13th, Part 2
rt_critics_consensus: Friday the 13th Part 2 sets the template for the franchise to follow with more teen victims, more gruesome set pieces, and fewer reasons to keep following along.

title: Tigers Are Not Afraid
rt_critics_consensus: Tigers Are Not Afraid draws on childhood trauma for a story that deftly blends magical fantasy and hard-hitting realism - and leaves a lingering impact.

title: Return of the Living Dead: Necropolis
Willy's Wonderland
rt_critics_consensus: Willy's Wonderland isn't quite as much fun as its premise would suggest -- but it's still got Nicolas Cage beating the hell out of bloodthirsty animatronics, which is nice.

title: The Bloody Exorcism of Coffin Joe
Wildling
rt_critics_consensus: Wildling's feminist themes - and the ferocious Bel Powley performance that brings them to life - are often enough to make up for its narrative deficiencies.

title: Soul Survivors
rt_critics_consensus: Soul Survivors' stock characters and utter lack of suspense gives viewers little reason to attempt deciphering the confusing plot.

title: Old
rt_critics_consensus: Old has no shortage of interesting ideas -- and writer-director M. Night Shyamalan's uneven execution will intrigue or annoy viewers, with little middle ground between.

title: Traumatika
rt_critics_consensus: Traumatika offers stylish, dread-soaked horror with harrowing visuals and sound, but its scattered plotting can make it feel like a collage of nightmares rather than a fully formed narrative.

title: The Year of the Plague (El año de la plaga)
Winnie-the-Pooh: Blood and Honey
rt_critics_consensus: Oh, bother.

title: Ghost of the Golden Groves (Shonajhurir Bhoot)
Kaidan hebi-onna (Snake Woman's Curse) (Ghost Story of the Snake Woman)
Haunters: The Art of the Scare
The Retaliators
rt_critics_consensus: It may not offer much they haven't seen before, but strong-stomached exploitation enthusiasts will savor The Retaliators.

title: Alice, Sweet Alice
rt_critics_consensus: Alice, Sweet Alice offsets some of its more ludicrous elements with campy slasher thrills and a dazzlingly macabre atmosphere.

title: Altered States
rt_critics_consensus: Extraordinarily daring for a Hollywood film, Altered States attacks the viewer with its inventive, aggressive mix of muddled sound effects and visual pyrotechnics.

title: Halloween Ends
rt_critics_consensus: Halloween Ends -- for now, anyway -- with a frequently befuddling installment that's stabbed, slashed, and beaten by a series of frustrating missed opportunities.

title: The Parenting
rt_critics_consensus: Playing in the well-worn horror-comedy sandbox, Craig Johnson's The Parenting may need a time-out when it comes to reinvigorating the genre.

title: Ju-On: The Beginning of the End
Genuine: The Tragedy of a Vampire
Astro Zombies: M4 - Invaders From Cyberspace
The Mad Doctor of Market Street
The Son of Raw's the Roman
Jakob's Wife
rt_critics_consensus: Jakob's Wife gives genre legend Barbara Crampton an opportunity to carry an old-school horror story -- and she bloody well delivers.

title: Saw V
rt_critics_consensus: If its plot were as interesting as its torture devices, or its violence less painful than its performances, perhaps Saw V might not feel like it was running on fumes.

title: Only Human
rt_critics_consensus: A cheerfully energetic screwball comedy, Only Human milks laughs from its culture-clash premise.

title: Quando Alice Ruppe lo Specchio (Touch of Death) (When Alice Broke the Mirror)
Stopmotion
rt_critics_consensus: Stopmotion takes the conflict between art and artist to chilling, visually thrilling extremes, distinguished by director Robert Morgan's excellent effects work.

title: Doctor S Battles the Sex Crazed Reefer Zombies: The Movie
Mutt
rt_critics_consensus: A slow-burning cinematic revelation, Mutt sees director Vuk Lunglov-Klotz navigating personal transition through a comprehensive lens.

title: Remington and the Curse of the Zombadings
Doom
rt_critics_consensus: The FPS sections are sure to please fans of the video game, but lacking in plot and originality to please other moviegoers.

title: Amen
rt_critics_consensus: Amen loses some of its impact to a protracted running time and deliberate pace, but Ulrich Tukur's powerful performance helps bring the story's worthy themes to life.

title: The Last House in the Woods
The Wrath of Becky
rt_critics_consensus: Bringing Lulu Wilson back for another round of whooping on bad guys, this Wrath is one Becky with the good sequel.

title: The Facts in the Case of Mister Hollow
Hubie Halloween
rt_critics_consensus: Viewers immune to its star's charms won't find it much of a treat, but Hubie Halloween is sweet enough to satisfy fans of Adam Sandler's antics.

title: A Ripper In Canada: Paranormal Hauntings in the Great White North
Seoul Station
rt_critics_consensus: Thrilling and relentless from start to finish, Yeon Sang-ho's Seoul Station is a layered and vicious entry into the zombie genre.

title: Return of the Beast From Outer Space
Scream Queen Hot Tub Party (Hollywood Scream Queen Hot Tub Party)
The Watchers: The Beginning of Sorrows
The Jack in the Box: Rises
Bamboozled
rt_critics_consensus: Bamboozled is too heavy-handed in its satire and comes across as more messy and overwrought than biting.

title: I Left My Heart in Colombia
Dave Made a Maze
rt_critics_consensus: Dave Made a Maze offers decent storytelling but plunges deep into bold stylistic waters, establishing director Bill Watterson as a fresh and inventive filmmaker.

title: The Ward
rt_critics_consensus: Lacking the hallmarks of his best work, The Ward proves to be a disappointingly mundane swan song for director John Carpenter.

title: The Dead and the Damned 3: Ravaged
Ten
rt_critics_consensus: Ten turns a conversational car ride into a gritty and compelling character study full of real emotion while providing an intriguing look into the lives of women in contemporary Iranian culture.

title: Cocoon
rt_critics_consensus: Though it may be too sentimental for some, Ron Howard's supernatural tale of eternal youth is gentle and heartwarming, touching on poignant issues of age in the process.

title: Shelby Oaks
rt_critics_consensus: Shelby Oaks may stumble in its final moments, but its atmospheric tension and emotional core firmly establish it as a chilling debut from director Chris Stuckmann.

title: Willow Creek
rt_critics_consensus: Writer-director Bobcat Goldthwait's first foray into horror doesn't break any new ground, but it does wring fresh terror from a well-worn genre formula -- and offers a few nasty laughs in the bargain.

title: Moon Garden
rt_critics_consensus: If writer-director Ryan Stevens Harris' story is somewhat less compelling than his bizarrely transfixing visuals, Moon Garden remains a mind-bending achievement of dark DIY fantasy.

title: Dementer
rt_critics_consensus: Although it sometimes struggles to make sense of its story, Dementer presents a unique -- and chillingly dark -- perspective on healthcare horror.

title: Dracula: Pages From a Virgin's Diary
rt_critics_consensus: Guy Maddin's film is a richly sensuous and dreamy interpretation of Dracula that reinvigorates the genre.

title: Daniel Isn't Real
rt_critics_consensus: Daniel Isn't Real, but the smart, stylish fun waiting for genre lovers in this well-acted suspense thriller is completely genuine.

title: The Midnight Meat Train
rt_critics_consensus: A creative and energetic adaptation of a Clive Barker short story, with enough scares and thrills to be a potential cult classic.

title: Belén
rt_critics_consensus: Belén is a gripping, fast-paced legal drama that elevates a familiar courtroom narrative into an urgent, deeply human call for justice through its richly human performances and searing emotional honesty.

title: Satan Wants You
rt_critics_consensus: Capturing a media frenzy in shocking, eye-opening fashion, this documentary has its eyes on the truth and a commitment to counter the fearmongering spread by some who believe Satan Wants You.

title: Titus
rt_critics_consensus: The movie stretches too long to be entertaining despite a strong cast.

title: Exorcist II: The Heretic
rt_critics_consensus: Hokey mystical effects, lousy plotting, and worse acting directly tarnishes the first's chilling legacy.

title: Something Wicked This Way Comes
rt_critics_consensus: True terror and typical Disney wholesomeness clash uncomfortably in Something Wicked This Way Comes.

title: Resident Evil: Afterlife
rt_critics_consensus: As dim-witted and lifeless as its undead antagonists, Resident Evil: Afterlife is a wholly unnecessary addition to the franchise.

title: Under the Shadow
rt_critics_consensus: Under the Shadow deftly blends seemingly disparate genres to deliver an effective chiller with timely themes and thought-provoking social subtext.

title: Life After Beth
rt_critics_consensus: In spite of Aubrey Plaza's committed performance, Life After Beth remains a sketch-worthy idea that's been uncomfortably stretched to feature length.

title: Quatermass and the Pit (Five Million Years to Earth)(The Mind Benders)
The Rite
rt_critics_consensus: Anthony Hopkins is as excellent as ever, but he's no match for The Rite's dawdling pace and lack of chills -- or Colin O'Donoghue's tentative performance in the leading role.

title: 1BR
rt_critics_consensus: 1BR's occasionally ordinary storytelling is more than outweighed by tight direction, interesting ideas, and an effective blend of horror and thoughtful drama.

title: Welcome Week: A College Horror Anthology
Where the Dead Go to Die
The Strange Case of Dr. Rx
Pride
rt_critics_consensus: Pride features a typically stellar performance from Terrence Howard, but ultimately falls victim to its over usage of sports movie clichés.

title: The Spine of Night
rt_critics_consensus: With a hard fantasy story that stands in service of its eye-catching animation, The Spine of Night is a distinctive treat for genre enthusiasts.

title: Ghosts of War
rt_critics_consensus: Ghosts of War blends supernatural horror and period war drama to produce a muddled mashup that proves some ingredients are better left separate.

title: Blood Feast 2: All U Can Eat
Ick
rt_critics_consensus: Slightly stuck in genre convention, but mostly an Ick-fest of horrific fun that oozes solid B-film trappings.

title: Dwellers: The Curse of Pastor Stokes
Chop Shop
rt_critics_consensus: Filled with excellent performances, Ramin Bahrani's deft sophomore effort is a heartfelt, hopeful neorealist look at the people who live in the gritty underbelly of New York City.

title: Bloodbath at the House of Death
Night's End
rt_critics_consensus: Flashes of suspense hint at the horror movie it might have been, but Night's End is ultimately too dull and repetitious to recommend.

title: The Girl With All the Gifts
rt_critics_consensus: The Girl with All the Gifts grapples with thought-provoking questions without skimping on the scares -- and finds a few fresh wrinkles in the well-worn zombie horror genre along the way.

title: I Married a Monster From Outer Space
Take Shelter
rt_critics_consensus: Michael Shannon gives a powerhouse performance and the purposefully subtle filmmaking creates a perfect blend of drama, terror, and dread.

title: Invisible: The Chronicles of Benjamin Knight
Scream 3
rt_critics_consensus: Despite some surprising twists, Scream 3 sees the franchise falling back on the same old horror formulas and cliches it once hacked and slashed with postmodern abandon.

title: Senritsu Kaiki File Super Kowa Too! Dark Mystery: Snake Woman
Holes
rt_critics_consensus: Faithful to its literary source, this is imaginative, intelligent family entertainment.

title: Bloody Oranges
rt_critics_consensus: A mixed bag of citrus, Bloody Oranges delivers a basket of scabrous black humor and provocative violence to those who enjoy WTF fare with a French flair.

title: Tone-Deaf
rt_critics_consensus: A messy horror-comedy hybrid that makes poor use of a talented cast, Tone-Deaf aims for social commentary, but tumbles down the generation gap.

title: Paranormal Extremes: Text Messages From the Dead
Three Kings
rt_critics_consensus: Three Kings successfully blends elements of action, drama, and comedy into a thoughtful, exciting movie on the Gulf War.

title: Di Renjie Secret Soldier Borrows the Road
Bram Stoker's Dracula
rt_critics_consensus: Overblown in the best sense of the word, Francis Ford Coppola's vision of Bram Stoker's Dracula rescues the character from decades of campy interpretations -- and features some terrific performances to boot.

title: Mega Shark vs. Giant Octopus
rt_critics_consensus: With shoddy FX, acting and directing. This isn't so bad it's good. It's just so bad it's terrible.

title: Return to the Theatre of Terror
Sovereign
rt_critics_consensus: A nerve-jangling thriller that exposes how reactionary extremism can lead to a road of destruction, Sovereign is ruled by Nick Offerman's unforgettably unhinged performance.

title: Pandorum
rt_critics_consensus: While it might prove somewhat satisfying for devout sci-fi fans, Pandorum's bloated, derivative plot ultimately leaves it drifting in space.

title: X Knight: Escape From Warp Hell
The Conjuring: Last Rites
rt_critics_consensus: Purportedly Vera Farmiga and Patrick Wilson's final outing as the Warrens, Last Rites underwhelms as a climactic chapter for The Conjuring but solidly upholds the franchise's spooky standards.

title: The Adventures of Young Van Helsing: Quest for the Lost Scepter
Signs
rt_critics_consensus: With Signs, Shyamalan proves once again an expert at building suspense and giving audiences the chills.

title: Beyond the Sea
rt_critics_consensus: Kevin Spacey's bio of singer Bobby Darin is either a fearless piece of showmanship or an embarrassing vanity project, according to critics.

title: Rusted Body: Guts of a Virgin III
It's a Wonderful Knife
rt_critics_consensus: It's a Wonderful Knife takes an enthusiastic stab at holiday-themed horror-comedy, even if it doesn't cut quite as deep as it should.

title: The Man Who Came to Kill
Tiger Stripes
rt_critics_consensus: A clever and stylish riff on the body horror that is puberty, Tiger Stripes is an exuberant allegory for personal agency as well as an impressive debut for writer-director Amanda Nell Eu.

title: Raging Grace
rt_critics_consensus: Dispensing social commentary shrouded in gothic dread, Raging Grace smartly balances its messaging against vivid atmosphere and absorbing character-driven drama.

title: Once Upon A Time In Amityville
Kids vs. Aliens
rt_critics_consensus: Kids vs. Aliens occasionally comes close to offering the goofy fun promised by the title, although the end result is ultimately rather forgettable.

title: Tale of Tales
rt_critics_consensus: Visually splendid and narratively satisfying, Tale of Tales packs an off-kilter wallop for mature viewers in search of something different.

title: The Haunted House on Kirby Road
Zombeavers
rt_critics_consensus: Zombeavers obviously isn't high art, but it delivers just enough of the gory insanity promised by the title to earn a recommendation for genre fans.

title: The Girl Who Cried Her Eyes Out
Lovely, Dark, and Deep
rt_critics_consensus: Effectively playing on fundamental fears, Lovely, Dark, and Deep is a creepy calling card for debuting director Teresa Sutherland that's further distinguished by Georgina Campbell's haunting performance.

title: Devil Dog: The Hound of Hell
Lemora: A Child's Tale of the Supernatural
The Haunting of the Morgan Estate
If It's a Man, Hang Up
15 Things You Didn't Know About Bigfoot (Number 1 Will Blow Your Mind!)
Henry: Portrait of a Serial Killer 2, Mask of Sanity
Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
rt_critics_consensus: Michael Keaton's devious poltergeist still has plenty of juice left in this madcap return to form for Tim Burton, marrying charming practical effects and ghoulish gags to provide a fun fun time.

title: Cockneys vs Zombies
rt_critics_consensus: If you're interested in watching a battle between cockneys and zombies, this is the movie for you.

title: The Devil Inside
rt_critics_consensus: The Devil Inside is a cheap, choppy unscary mess, featuring one of the worst endings in recent memory.

title: H6: Diary of a Serial Killer
Black Phone 2
rt_critics_consensus: Taking the consequences of its predecessor seriously, Black Phone 2 is a chilling sequel that mines memorable scares and thought-provoking themes from the scars that linger.

title: The Room Next Door
rt_critics_consensus: Anchored by a pair of terrific performances swathed in vivid colors, Spanish auteur Pedro Almodóvar's English-language feature debut attests to his universal fluency in provocative filmmaking.
